17.7: He filled them with the knowledge of wisdom || And showed them good and evil.
17.8: He set His eye on their hearts, || To show them the majesty of His works.
17.9: And He allowed them to take pride forever in His marvelous deeds.
17.10: And they will praise the Name of His holiness, || That they may declare the majesty of His works.
17.11: He added to them knowledge || And gave them a law of life for a heritage.
17.12: He made a perpetual covenant with them || And showed them His judgments.
17.13: Their eyes saw the majesty of His glory. Their ears heard the glory of His voice.
17.14: He said to them, “Beware of all unrighteousness.” So He gave them command—Each man concerning his neighbor.
17.15: Their ways are ever before Him. They will not be hidden from His eyes.
17.16: From childhood on they tend to be evil; Their heart is like stone, || And they do not seem to be able to make it more human.
17.17: He divided the nations of the whole earth. For every nation He appointed a ruler, || But Israel is the Lord ’s portion.
17.18: Israel is His firstborn, || Whom He disciplines as He brings him up. He gives him the light of His love and never neglects him.
17.19: All their works are as clear as the sun before Him. His eyes are continually on their ways.
17.20: Their iniquities are not hidden from Him. All their sins are before the Lord.
17.21: But the Lord is gracious and knows His creatures; So He has spared them rather than abandon them.
17.22: With Him the kindness of a man is as a signet. He will keep the bounty of a man as the apple of the eye.
17.23: Afterward He will rise up, and repay them, || And render their repayment on their head.
17.24: However, to those who convert He grants a return. He comforts those who are losing patience.
17.25: Return to the Lord and forsake sins. Make your prayer before His face offend less.
17.26: Turn to the Most High again || And turn away from iniquity. Greatly hate the abominable thing.
17.27: Who will give praise to the Most High in Hades, || In place of those who live and return thanks?
17.28: Thanksgiving perishes from the dead, || As from one who does not exist. He who is in life and health will praise the Lord.
17.29: How great is the mercy of the Lord, || And His forgiveness to those who turn to Him!
17.30: For all things cannot be in men, || Because the son of man is not immortal.
17.31: What is brighter than the sun? Yet even this fails. An evil man thinks about flesh and blood.
17.32: He looks on the power of the height of Heaven, || While all men are earth and ashes.

18.1: He who lives forever created all things in common.
18.2: The Lord alone will be justified || And there is no other besides Him.
18.3: He guides the world with His hand, || And everything obeys Him. He is the King of all things, || And His power separates what is holy from what is not.
18.4: He has given power to declare His works to no one. Who could trace out His mighty deeds?
18.5: Who could measure the strength of His majesty? Who could also proclaim His mercies?
18.6: As for the wondrous works of the Lord, || It is not possible to take from them nor add to them, || Neither is it possible to explore them.
18.7: When a man has finished, || Then he is just at the beginning. When he ceases, || Then he will be in perplexity.
18.8: What is mankind, and what purpose do they serve? What is their good, and what is their evil?
18.9: The number of man’s days at the most are one hundred years.
18.10: As a drop of water from the sea, and a pebble from the sand, || So are a few years in the day [[ or time ]] of [the] age.
18.11: For this reason the Lord was patient over them || And poured out His mercy on them.
18.12: He saw and perceived their end, || That it is evil. Therefore, He multiplied His forgiveness.
18.13: The mercy of a man is on his neighbor; But the mercy of the Lord is on all flesh: Reproving, disciplining, teaching, and bringing back, || As a shepherd does his flock.
18.14: He has mercy on those who accept disciplining, || And that diligently seek after His judgments.
18.15: My son, add no blemish to your good deeds, || And no grief of words in any of your giving.
18.16: Does the dew not relieve the scorching heat? So a word is better than a gift.
18.17: Behold, is a word not better than a gift? Both are with a gracious man.
18.18: A fool is ungracious and abusive. The gift of an envious man consumes the eyes.
18.19: Learn before you speak. Take care of your health before you are sick.
18.20: Before judgment examine yourself, || And in the hour of visitation you will find forgiveness.
18.21: Humble yourself before you become sick. In the time of sins, show conversion.
18.22: Let nothing hinder you to pay your vow in due time. Do not wait until death to be justified.
18.23: Before you make a vow, prepare yourself. Do not be like a man who tempts the Lord.
18.24: Think about the wrath coming in the days of the end, || And the time of vengeance, when He turns away His face.
18.25: In the days of fullness remember the time of hunger. Remember poverty and lack in the days of wealth.
18.26: From morning until evening, the time changes. All things are speedy before the Lord.
18.27: A wise man is cautious in everything. In days of sinning, he will beware of offense.
18.28: Every man of understanding knows wisdom. He will give thanks to him who found her.
18.29: They who were of understanding in sayings || Also became wise themselves and poured out apt proverbs.
18.30: Do not go after your lusts. Refrain yourself from your appetites.
18.31: If you give fully to your soul the delight of her desire, || She will make you the laughing-stock of your enemies.
18.32: Do not make merry in much luxury, || Neither be tied to the expense thereof.
18.33: Do not be made a beggar by banqueting on borrowing || When you have nothing in your purse.

19.1: A workman who is a drunkard will not become rich. He who despises small things will fall little by little.
19.2: Wine and women will make men of understanding fall away. And he who joins with prostitutes will be more reckless.
19.3: Moths and worms will have him as their heritage. A reckless soul will be taken away.
19.4: He who is hasty to trust is shallow-minded. He who sins will offend against his own soul.
19.5: He who makes merry in his heart will be condemned:
19.6: He who hates talk has less wickedness.
19.7: Never repeat what is told you, || And you will not be lacking.
19.8: Whether it is of friend or enemy, do not tell it. Unless it is a sin to you, do not reveal it.
19.9: For he has heard you and observed you, || And when the time comes, he will hate you.
19.10: Have you heard a word? Let it die with you. Be of good courage: it will not burst you.
19.11: A fool will travail in pain with a word, || As a woman in labor with a child.
19.12: As an arrow that sticks in the flesh of the thigh, || So is a word in a fool’s belly.
19.13: Reprove a friend: It may be [that] he did not do it. If he did something, || It may be [that] he may not do it again.
19.14: Reprove your neighbor: It may be [that] he did not say it. If he has said it, || It may be [that] he may not say it again.
19.15: Reprove a friend: For many times there is slander. Do not trust every word.
19.16: There is one who slips, and not from the heart. Who is he who has not sinned with his tongue?
19.17: Reprove your neighbor before you threaten him; And give place to the Law of the Most High; And do not be angry.
19.18: Fearing the Lord is the first step toward His accepting you; He will love you if you are wise.
19.19: Learn the Lord ’s commands. It is a discipline that gives life. Those who do what pleases Him || Enjoy the fruit of the tree of immortality.
19.20: All wisdom is the fear of the Lord. In all wisdom is the doing of the Law || And what the knowledge of His omnipotence means.
19.21: If a servant refuses to obey his master, but later does obey, || The master is still angry.
19.22: The knowledge of wickedness is not wisdom. The prudence of sinners is not counsel.
19.23: There is a wickedness, and it is abomination. There is a fool lacking in wisdom.
19.24: Better is one who has small understanding, and fears, || Than one who has much prudence, and transgresses the Law.
19.25: There is an exquisite subtlety, and it is unjust. And there is one who perverts favor to gain a judgment.
19.26: There is one who does wickedly, || Who hangs down his head with mourning, || But inwardly he is full of deceit,
19.27: Bowing his face down, || And making [it seem] as if he were deaf of one ear. Where he is not known, || He will be beforehand with you.
19.28: And if for lack of power he is hindered from sinning, || If he finds opportunity, he will do mischief.
19.29: A man will be known by his look; One who has understanding will be known by his face when you meet him.
19.30: A man’s attire, grinning laughter, || And gait show what he is.

20.1: There is a reproof that is not timely; And there is a man who keeps silent and is wise.
20.2: How good it is to reprove, rather than to be angry. He who confesses will be kept back from hurt.
20.3: Admit when you are wrong, || And you will avoid embarrassment.
20.4: As is the lust of a eunuch to deflower a virgin, || So is he who executes judgments with violence.
20.5: There is one who keeps silent and is found wise; And there is one who is hated for his much talk.
20.6: There is one who keeps silent, || For he has no answer to make; And there is one who keeps silent, as knowing his time.
20.7: A wise man will be silent until his time has come, || But the braggart and fool will miss his time.
20.8: He who uses many words will be abhorred. He who takes authority for himself will be hated in it.
20.9: There is a prosperity that a man finds in misfortunes; And there is a gain that turns to loss.
20.10: There is a gift that will not profit you; And there is a gift [that] pays back double.
20.11: There is an abasement because of glory; And there is one who has lifted up his head from a low estate.
20.12: There is one who buys much for a little and pays for it again sevenfold.
20.13: He who is wise in words will make himself beloved; But the pleasantries of fools will be wasted.
20.14: The gift of a fool will not profit you; For his eyes are many instead of one.
20.15: He will give little and insult much. He will open his mouth like a crier. Today he will lend, || And tomorrow he will ask for it back. Such a one is a hateful man.
20.16: The fool will say, “I have no friend, || And I have no thanks for my good deeds. They who eat my bread are of evil tongue.”
20.17: How often, and of how many, will he be laughed to scorn!
20.18: A slip on a pavement is better than a slip with the tongue. So the fall of the wicked will come speedily.
20.19: A man without grace is a tale out of season. It will be continually in the mouth of the ignorant.
20.20: An allegory from a fool’s mouth will be rejected; For he will not speak it in its season.
20.21: There is one who is hindered from sinning through lack. When he rests, he will not be troubled.
20.22: There is one who destroys his soul through bashfulness. By a foolish countenance, he will destroy it.
20.23: There is one who for bashfulness makes promises to his friend; And he makes him his enemy for nothing.
20.24: A lie is a foul blot in a man. It will be continually in the mouth of the ignorant.
20.25: A thief is better than a man who is continually lying, || But they will both inherit destruction.
20.26: The disposition of a liar is dishonor. His shame is with him continually.
20.27: He who is wise in words will advance himself. And one who is prudent will please great men.
20.28: He who tills his land will raise his heap high. He who pleases great men will get pardon for iniquity.
20.29: Presents and gifts blind the eyes of the wise, || And as a muzzle on the mouth, turn away reproofs.
20.30: Wisdom that is hidden, and treasure that is out of sight—What profit is in them both?
20.31: Better is a man who hides his folly || Than a man who hides his wisdom.

21.1: My son, have you sinned? Add no more to it; And ask forgiveness for your past sins.
21.2: Flee from sin as from the face of a serpent; For if you draw near, it will bite you. Its teeth are lion’s teeth, slaying men’s souls.
21.3: All iniquity is as a two-edged sword. Its stroke has no healing.
21.4: Terror and violence will lay waste riches. So the house of an arrogant man will be laid waste.
21.5: Supplication from a poor man’s mouth reaches to the ears of God, || And his judgment comes speedily.
21.6: One who hates reproof is in the path of the sinner. He who fears the Lord will turn again in his heart.
21.7: He who is mighty in tongue is known far away, || But the man of understanding knows when he slips.
21.8: He who builds his house with other men’s money || Is like one who gathers stones for his own tomb.
21.9: The congregation of wicked men is as a bundle of tow || With a flame of fire at the end of them.
21.10: The way of sinners is paved with stones, || And at the end of it is the pit of Hades.
21.11: He who keeps the Law becomes master of its intent. The end of the fear of the Lord is wisdom.
21.12: He who is not clever will not be instructed. There is a cleverness which makes bitterness abound.
21.13: The knowledge of a wise man will be made to abound as a flood, || And his counsel as a fountain of life.
21.14: The inward parts of a fool are like a broken vessel. He will hold no knowledge.
21.15: If a man of knowledge hears a wise word, || He will commend it and add to it. The wanton man hears it, and it displeases him, || So he puts it away behind his back.
21.16: The discourse of a fool is like a burden in the way, || But grace will be found on the lips of the wise.
21.17: The mouth of the prudent man will be sought for in the congregation. They will ponder his words in their heart.
21.18: As a house that is destroyed, || So is wisdom to a fool. The knowledge of an unwise man is talk without sense.
21.19: Instruction is as chains on the feet of an unwise man, || And as shackles on the right hand.
21.20: A fool lifts up his voice with laughter, || But a clever man smiles quietly.
21.21: Instruction is to a prudent man as an ornament of gold, || And as a bracelet on his right arm.
21.22: The foot of a fool rushes into a house, || But a man of experience will be ashamed of entering.
21.23: A foolish man peers into the door of a house, || But a man who is instructed will stand outside.
21.24: It is a lack of instruction in a man to listen at a door, || But a prudent man will be grieved with the disgrace.
21.25: The lips of strangers will be grieved at these things, || But the words of prudent men will be weighed in the balance.
21.26: The heart of fools is in their mouth, || But the mouth of wise men is their heart.
21.27: When the ungodly curses Satan, || He curses his own soul.
21.28: A whisperer defiles his own soul || And will be hated wherever he travels.

22.1: A slothful man is compared to a stone that is defiled. Everyone will hiss at him in his disgrace.
22.2: A slothful man is compared to the filth of a dunghill. Every man who takes it up will shake out his hand.
22.3: An uninstructed child is a disgrace to his father, || And a foolish daughter is born to his loss.
22.4: A prudent daughter will inherit a husband of her own. She who brings shame is the grief of her father.
22.5: She who is bold brings shame on father and husband. She will be despised by both of them.
22.6: Unseasonable discourse is as music in mourning, || But stripes and correction are wisdom in every season.
22.7: He who teaches a fool is as one who glues a potsherd together, || Even as one who wakes a sleeper out of a deep sleep.
22.8: He who teaches a fool is as one who teaches a man who slumbers. In the end he will say, “What is it?”
22.9: Children who are brought up well || Do not show the humble origin of their parents.
22.10: Children who are not brought up well, || Who are arrogant and conceited, || Are a stain on the noblest family.
22.11: Weep for the dead, for he lacks light. Weep for a fool, for he lacks understanding. Weep more sweetly for the dead, because he has found rest, || But the life of the fool is worse than death.
22.12: Mourning for the dead lasts seven days, || But for a fool and an ungodly man, all the days of his life.
22.13: Do not talk much with a foolish man, || And do not go to one that has no understanding: Beware of him, lest you have trouble and be defiled in his onslaught. Turn away from him, and you will find rest, || And you will not be wearied in his madness.
22.14: What would be heavier than lead? What is its name, but a fool?
22.15: Sand, salt, and a mass of iron are easier to bear || Than a man without understanding.
22.16: Timber girded and bound into a building || Will not be released with shaking. So a heart established in due season on well advised counsel || Will not be afraid.
22.17: A heart settled on a thoughtful understanding || Is as an ornament of plaster on a polished wall.
22.18: Fences set on a high place || Will not stand against the wind; So a fearful heart in the imagination of a fool || Will not stand against any fear.
22.19: He who pricks the eye will make tears fall. He who pricks the heart makes it show feeling.
22.20: Whoever casts a stone at birds scares them away. He who insults a friend will dissolve friendship.
22.21: If you have drawn a sword against a friend, do not despair, || For there may be a way back.
22.22: If you have opened your mouth against a friend, do not be afraid, || For there may be reconciliation, || Unless it is for insulting, arrogance, disclosing of a secret, or a treacherous blow—For any friend will flee these things.
22.23: Gain trust with your neighbor in his poverty, || That in his prosperity you may have gladness. Stay steadfast to him in the time of his affliction, || That you may be heir with him in his inheritance.
22.24: Before fire is the vapor and smoke of a furnace, || So insults precede bloodshed.
22.25: I will not be ashamed to shelter a friend. I will not hide myself from his face:
22.26: If any evil happens to me because of him, || Everyone that hears it will beware of him.
22.27: Who will set a watch over my mouth, || And a seal of shrewdness on my lips, || That I do not fall from it, || And that my tongue does not destroy me?

23.1: O Lord, Father and Master of my life, || Do not abandon me to their counsel. Do not let me fall by them.
23.2: Who will set scourges over my thought, || And a discipline of wisdom over my heart, || That they do not spare me for my errors, || And not overlook their [[ or my ]] sins?
23.3: Otherwise my errors might be multiplied, || And my sins abound, || [And] I fall before my adversaries, || And my enemy rejoice over me.
23.4: O Lord, Father and God of my life, || Do not give me haughty eyes,
23.5: And turn evil desire away from me.
23.6: Let neither gluttony nor lust overtake me. Do not give me over to a shameless mind.
23.7: Listen, my children, to the discipline of the mouth. He who keeps it will not be taken.
23.8: The sinner will be overpowered through his lips. By them, the insulter and the arrogant will stumble.
23.9: Do not accustom your mouth to an oath, || And do not be accustomed to naming the Holy One,
23.10: For as a servant who is continually scourged will not lack a bruise, || So he also who swears and continually utters the Name || Will not be cleansed from sin.
23.11: A man of many oaths will be filled with iniquity. The scourge will not depart from his house. If he offends, his sin will be on him. If he disregards it, he has sinned doubly. If he has sworn in vain, he will not be justified, || For his house will be filled with calamities.
23.12: There is a manner of speech that is clothed with death. Do not let it be found in the heritage of Jacob, || For all these things will be far from the godly, || And they will not wallow in sins.
23.13: Do not accustom your mouth to gross rudeness, || For it involves sinful speech.
23.14: Remember your father and your mother, || For you sit in the midst of great men, || That you are not forgetful before them, || And become a fool by your custom; So you may wish that you had not been born, || And curse the day of your nativity.
23.15: A man who is accustomed to words of reproach || Will not be corrected all the days of his life.
23.16: Two sorts of people multiply sins, || And the third will bring wrath: A hot mind, as a burning fire, || Will not be quenched until it is consumed; A fornicator, in the body of his flesh, || Will never cease until he has burned out the fire.
23.17: All bread is sweet to a fornicator. He will not cease until he dies.
23.18: A man who goes astray from his own bed, || Says in his heart, “Who sees me? Darkness is around me, and the walls hide me. No one sees me. Of whom am I afraid? The Most High will not remember my sins.”
23.19: The eyes of men are his terror. He does not know that the eyes of the Lord || Are ten thousand times brighter than the sun, || Seeing all the ways of men, || And looking into secret places.
23.20: All things were known to Him before they were created, || And also after they were completed.
23.21: This man will be punished in the streets of the city. He will be seized where he least expects it.
23.22: So also is a wife who leaves her husband || And brings in an heir by a stranger.
23.23: For first, she was disobedient in the Law of the Most High. Second, she trespassed against her own husband. Third, she played the adulteress in whoredom, || And brought in children by a stranger.
23.24: She will be brought out into the congregation. Her punishment will extend to her children.
23.25: Her children will not take root. Her branches will bear no fruit.
23.26: She will leave her memory for a curse. Her reproach will not be blotted out.
23.27: And those who are left behind || Will know that there is nothing better than the fear of the Lord, || And nothing sweeter than to heed the commands of the Lord.

24.1: Wisdom will praise her own soul || And will proclaim her glory in the midst of her people.
24.2: She will open her mouth in the congregation of the Most High || And proclaim her glory in the presence of His power.
24.3: “I came out of the mouth of the Most High || And covered the earth as a mist.
24.4: I lived in high places, || And my throne is in the pillar of the cloud.
24.5: Alone I surrounded the circuit of Heaven || And walked in the depth of the abyss.
24.6: In the waves of the sea, and in all the earth, || And in every people and nation, I got a possession.
24.7: With all these I sought rest. In whose inheritance will I lodge?
24.8: Then the Creator of all things gave me a command. He who created me made my dwelling place to rest, || And said, Let your dwelling place be in Jacob, || And your inheritance in Israel.
24.9: He created me from the beginning before the world. For all ages, I will not cease to exist.
24.10: In the holy Dwelling Place, I ministered before Him. So I was established in Zion.
24.11: In the beloved city, likewise He gave me rest. In Jerusalem was my domain.
24.12: I took root in a people that was glorified, || Even in the portion of the Lord ’s own inheritance.
24.13: I was exalted like a cedar in Lebanon, || And like a cypress tree on the mountains of Hermon.
24.14: I was exalted like a palm tree on the seashore, || And as rose plants in Jericho, || And as a fair olive tree in the plain. I was exalted as a plane tree.
24.15: As cinnamon and as palathus, || I have given a scent to perfumes. As choice myrrh, I spread abroad a pleasant fragrance, || As galbanum, onyx, stacte, || And as the smell of frankincense in the Dwelling Place.
24.16: As the terebinth, I stretched out my branches. My branches are branches of glory and grace.
24.17: As the vine, I put out grace. My flowers are the fruit of glory and riches.
24.18: I am the mother of beautiful love, || Of fear, knowledge, and holy hope. Since I am perpetual, || I am given to all my children, who are named by Him.
24.19: Come to me, all you who desire me, || And be filled with my fruits.
24.20: For my memorial is sweeter than honey, || And my inheritance [is sweeter] than the honeycomb.
24.21: Those who eat me will be hungry for more. Those who drink me will be thirsty for more.
24.22: He who obeys me will not be ashamed. Those who work in me will not sin.”
24.23: All these things are the Scroll of the Covenant of the Most High God, || The Law which Moses commanded us for an inheritance for the assemblies of Jacob.
24.24: Always be strong in the Lord; Stay with Him, so that He may make you strong. There is no God but the Lord Almighty, || And no savior except Him.
24.25: It is He who makes wisdom abundant, || As Pishon, and as Tigris in the days of first-fruits.
24.26: He makes understanding as full as [the] Euphrates, || And as [the] Jordan in the days of harvest,
24.27: Who makes instruction shine out as the light, || As [the] Gihon in the days of vintage.
24.28: The first man did not know her perfectly. In like manner, the last has not explored her.
24.29: For her thoughts are filled from the sea, || And her counsels from the great deep.
24.30: “I came out as a canal stream from a river, || And as an irrigation ditch into a garden.
24.31: I said, I will water my garden, || And will drench my garden bed. Behold, my stream became a river, || And my river became a sea.
24.32: I will yet bring instruction to light as the morning || And will make these things clear from far away.
24.33: I will continue to pour out doctrine like prophecy || And leave it to generations of ages.
24.34: Behold that I have not labored for myself only, || But for all those who diligently seek her.”

25.1: In three things I was beautified || And stood up beautiful before the Lord and men: The concord of relatives, || And friendship of neighbors, || And a woman and her husband that walk together in agreement.
25.2: But three sorts [of men] my soul hates, || And I am greatly offended at their life: A poor man that is arrogant, || And a rich man that is a liar, || [And] an old man that is an adulterer lacking understanding.
25.3: In [your] youth you have not gathered, || And how should you find in your old age?
25.4: How beautiful a thing is judgment for gray hairs, || And for elders to know counsel!
25.5: How beautiful is the wisdom of old men, || And thought and counsel to men that are in honor!
25.6: Much experience is the crown of old men; And their glorying is the fear of the Lord.
25.7: There are nine things that I have thought of, || And in my heart counted happy; And the tenth I will utter with my tongue: A man that rejoices in his children; A man that lives and looks on the fall of his enemies;
25.8: Happy is he that dwells with a wife of understanding; And he that has not slipped with his tongue; And he that has not served a man that is unworthy of him;
25.9: Happy is he that has found prudence; And he that speaks in the ears of those who listen.
25.10: How great is he that has found wisdom! Yet there is none above him that fears the Lord.
25.11: The fear of the Lord passes all things: He that holds it—To whom will he be likened?
25.12: Fearing the Lord is the first step toward loving Him, || And faith is the first step toward loyalty to Him.
25.13: [Give me] any plague but the plague of the heart; And any wickedness but the wickedness of a woman;
25.14: Any calamity, but a calamity from those who hate me; And any vengeance, but the vengeance of enemies.
25.15: There is no head above the head of a serpent; And there is no wrath above the wrath of an enemy.
25.16: I would rather dwell with a lion and a dragon, || Than keep house with a wicked woman.
25.17: The wickedness of a woman changes her look || And darkens her countenance as a bear does.
25.18: Her husband will sit at meat among his neighbors, || And when he hears it he sighs bitterly.
25.19: All malice is but little to the malice of a woman: Let the portion of a sinner fall on her.
25.20: [As] the going up a sandy way [is] to the feet of the aged, || So is a wife full of words to a quiet man.
25.21: Do not throw yourself on the beauty of a woman; And do not desire a woman for her beauty.
25.22: There is anger, and impudence, and great reproach, || If a woman supports her husband.
25.23: A wicked woman is a dejection of heart, || And sadness of countenance, and a wounded heart: A woman that will not make her husband happy || Is [as] hands that hang down, and palsied knees.
25.24: From a woman [was] the beginning of sin; And because of her we all die.
25.25: Do not give water an outlet; Neither [give] freedom of speech to a wicked woman.
25.26: If she does not go as you would have her, || Cut her off from your flesh.

26.1: Happy is the husband of a good wife; And the number of his days will be doubled.
26.2: A brave woman rejoices her husband; And he will fulfill his years in peace.
26.3: A good wife is a good portion: She will be given in the portion of such as fear the Lord.
26.4: Whether a man is rich or poor, || A good heart [makes] a cheerful countenance at all times.
26.5: Of three things my heart was afraid; And concerning the fourth kind I made supplication: The slander of a city, || And the assembly of a multitude, || And a false accusation: All these are more grievous than death.
26.6: A grief of heart and sorrow is a woman that is jealous of [another] woman, || And the scourge of a tongue communicating to all.
26.7: A wicked woman is [as] a yoke of oxen shaken to and fro: He that takes hold of her is as one that grasps a scorpion.
26.8: A drunken woman [causes] great wrath; And she will not cover her own shame.
26.9: The whoredom of a woman is in the lifting up of her eyes; And it will be known by her eyelids.
26.10: Keep strict watch on a headstrong daughter, || Lest she finds liberty for herself, and uses it.
26.11: Look well after an impudent eye; And do not marvel if it trespasses against you.
26.12: She will open her mouth as a thirsty traveler, || And drink of every water that is near: She will sit down at every post || And open her quiver against [any] arrow.
26.13: The grace of a wife will delight her husband; And her knowledge will fatten his bones.
26.14: A silent woman is a gift of the Lord; And there is nothing worth so much as a well-instructed soul.
26.15: A modest woman is grace on grace; And there is no price worthy of a continent soul.
26.16: As the sun when it arises in the highest places of the Lord, || So is the beauty of a good wife in the ordering of a man’s house.
26.17: As the lamp that shines on the holy candlestick, || So is the beauty of the face in ripe age.
26.18: As the golden pillars are on a base of silver, || So are beautiful feet with the breasts of one that is steadfast.
26.19: My child, stay healthy while you are young, || And do not give your strength to strangers.
26.20: Search the whole land for a fertile field, || And plant it with your own seed, || Trusting your own good stock.
26.21: Then your children will survive || And grow up confident of their good family.
26.22: A prostitute is like spit; A married woman who has affairs brings death to her lovers.
26.23: A lawless man will get a godless wife, as he deserves, || But a man who honors the Lord will have a devout wife.
26.24: A shameless wife enjoys making a disgrace of herself, || But a modest wife will act modestly even alone with her husband.
26.25: A self-willed woman is a dog, || But a woman with a sense of decency honors the Lord.
26.26: A wife who honors her husband will seem wise to everyone; But if she dishonors him by her overbearing attitude, || Everyone will know that she is ungodly. The husband of a good wife is fortunate, || Because he will live twice as long.
26.27: A loud-mouthed, talkative woman is like a trumpet sounding the signal for attack, || And any man who has such a wife will spend his life at war.
26.28: For two things my heart is grieved; And for the third anger comes on me: A man of war that suffers for poverty; And men of understanding that are counted as refuse; One that turns back from righteousness to sin— The Lord will prepare him for the sword.
26.29: A merchant will hardly keep himself from wrongdoing; And a huckster will not be acquitted of sin.

27.1: Many have sinned for a thing indifferent; And he that seeks to multiply [gain] will turn his eye away.
27.2: A nail will stick fast between the joining of stones; And sin will thrust itself in between buying and selling.
27.3: Unless [a man] holds on diligently in the fear of the Lord, || His house will soon be overthrown.
27.4: In the shaking of a sieve, the refuse remains; So is the filth of man in his reasoning.
27.5: The furnace will prove the potter’s vessels; And the trial of a man is in his reasoning.
27.6: The fruit of a tree declares the farming thereof; So is the utterance of the thought of the heart of a man.
27.7: Praise no man before [you hear him] reason; For this is the trial of men.
27.8: If you follow righteousness, you will obtain her, || And put her on, as a long robe of glory.
27.9: Birds will return to their like; And truth will return to those who practice her.
27.10: The lion lies in wait for prey; So does sin for those who work iniquity.
27.11: The discourse of a godly man is always wisdom, || But the foolish man changes as the moon.
27.12: Among men void of understanding observe the opportunity; But continually stay among the thoughtful.
27.13: The discourse of fools is an offense; And their laughter is in the wantonness of sin.
27.14: The talk of a man of many oaths will make the hair stand upright; And their strife makes one stop his ears.
27.15: The strife of the proud is a shedding of blood; And their reviling of each other is a grievous thing to hear.
27.16: He that reveals secrets destroys credit || And will not find a friend to his mind.
27.17: Love a friend and keep faith with him, || But if you reveal his secrets, || You will not pursue after him;
27.18: For as a man has destroyed his enemy, || So have you destroyed the friendship of your neighbor.
27.19: And as a bird which you have released out of your hand, || So have you let your neighbor go, || And you will not catch him again:
27.20: Do not pursue him, for he has gone far away, || And has escaped as a gazelle out of the snare.
27.21: For a wound may be bound up, || And after reviling there may be a reconcilement; But he that reveals secrets has lost hope.
27.22: One that winks with the eye contrives evil things; And no man will remove him from it.
27.23: When you are present, he will speak sweetly, || And will admire your words; But afterward he will writhe his mouth || And set a trap [for you] in your words.
27.24: I have hated many things, || But nothing like him; And the Lord will hate him.
27.25: One that casts a stone on high casts it on his own head; And a deceitful stroke will open wounds.
27.26: He that digs a pit will fall into it; And he that sets a snare will be taken therein.
27.27: He that does evil things, they will roll on him, || And he will not know from where they have come to him.
27.28: Mockery and reproach are from the arrogant; And vengeance, as a lion, will lie in wait for him.
27.29: They that rejoice at the fall of the godly || Will be taken in a snare; And anguish will consume them before they die.
27.30: Wrath and anger, these are also abominations; And a sinful man will possess them.

28.1: He that takes vengeance will find vengeance from the Lord; And he will surely make firm his sins.
28.2: Forgive your neighbor the hurt that he has done [you]; And then your sins will be pardoned when you pray.
28.3: Man cherishes anger against man; And does he [then] seek healing from the Lord?
28.4: On a man like himself he has no mercy; And does he [then] make supplication for his own sins?
28.5: He being himself flesh nourishes wrath: Who will make atonement for his sins?
28.6: Remember your last end, || And cease from enmity: [Remember] corruption and death, || And remain in the commands.
28.7: Remember the commands || And do not be angry with your neighbor; And [remember] the covenant of the Highest, || And wink at ignorance.
28.8: Abstain from strife, and you will diminish your sins, || For a passionate man will kindle strife;
28.9: And a man that is a sinner will trouble friends || And will make debate among those who are at peace.
28.10: As is the fuel of the fire, so will it burn; And as the stoutness of the strife is, [so] will it burn; As is the strength of the man, [so] will be his wrath; And as is his wealth, [so] he will exalt his anger.
28.11: A contention begun in haste kindles a fire; And a hasty fighting sheds blood.
28.12: If you blow a spark, it will burn; And if you spit on it, it will be quenched: And both these will come out of your mouth.
28.13: Curse the whisperer and double-tongued, || For he has destroyed many that were at peace.
28.14: A third person’s tongue has shaken many || And dispersed them from nation to nation; And it has pulled down strong cities || And overthrown the houses of great men.
28.15: A third person’s tongue has cast out brave women || And deprived them of their labors.
28.16: He that listens to it will not find rest, || Nor will he dwell quietly.
28.17: The stroke of a whip makes a mark in the flesh; But the stroke of a tongue will break bones.
28.18: Many have fallen by the edge of the sword: Yet not so many as those who have fallen because of the tongue.
28.19: Happy is he that is sheltered from it, || Who has not passed through the wrath thereof; Who has not drawn its yoke, || And has not been bound with its bands.
28.20: For the yoke thereof is a yoke of iron, || And the bands thereof are bands of brass.
28.21: The death thereof is an evil death; And Hades would be better than it.
28.22: It will not have rule over godly men; And they will not be burned in its flame.
28.23: They that forsake the Lord will fall into it; And it will burn among them and will not be quenched: It will be sent out on them as a lion; And as a leopard it will destroy them.
28.24: See to it that you hedge around your possession with thorns; Bind up your silver and your gold;
28.25: And make a balance and a weight for your words; And make a door and a bar for your mouth.
28.26: Take heed lest you slip therein; Lest you fall before one that lies in wait.

29.1: He that shows mercy will lend to his neighbor; And he that strengthens him with his hand keeps the commands.
29.2: Lend to your neighbor in time of his need; And pay your neighbor again in due season.
29.3: Confirm your word and keep faith with him; And at all seasons you will find what you need.
29.4: Many have reckoned a loan as a windfall || And have given trouble to those that helped them.
29.5: Until he has received, he will kiss a man’s hands; And for his neighbor’s money he will speak submissively: And when payment is due, he will prolong the time, || And return words of heaviness, and complain of the times.
29.6: If he prevails, he will hardly receive the half; And he will count it as a windfall: If not, he has deprived him of his money, || And he has gotten him for an enemy without cause: He will pay him with cursing and railing; And he will pay him disgrace for honor.
29.7: Many on account of [men’s] ill-dealing have turned away; They have feared to be defrauded for nothing.
29.8: However, be patient with a man in poor estate; And do not let him wait for [your] kindness.
29.9: Help a poor man for the command’s sake; And according to his need, do not send him away empty.
29.10: Lose [your] money for a brother and a friend; And do not let it rust under the stone to be lost.
29.11: Bestow your treasure according to the commands of the Most High, || And it will profit you more than gold.
29.12: Shut up alms in your store-chambers, || And it will deliver you out of all affliction:
29.13: It will fight for you against your enemy || Better than a mighty shield and a ponderous spear.
29.14: A good man will be a guarantee for his neighbor; And he that has lost shame will fail him.
29.15: Do not forget the good offices of your guarantor; For he has given his life for you.
29.16: A sinner will overthrow the good estate of his guarantor;
29.17: And he that is of an unthankful mind will fail him that delivered him.
29.18: Suretyship has undone many that were prospering || And shaken them as a wave of the sea: It has driven mighty men from their homes; And they wandered among strange nations.
29.19: A sinner that falls into suretyship, || And undertakes contracts for work, || Will fall into lawsuits.
29.20: Help your neighbor according to your power || And take heed to yourself that you do not fall [the same way].
29.21: The chief thing for life is water, and bread, || And a garment, and a house to cover shame.
29.22: Better is the life of a poor man under a shelter of logs, || Than sumptuous fare in another man’s house.
29.23: With little or with much, be well satisfied.
29.24: It is a miserable life to go from house to house: And where you are a sojourner, || You will not [dare to] open your mouth.
29.25: You will entertain, and give to drink, and have no thanks: And in addition to this, you will hear bitter words.
29.26: “Come here, you sojourner, furnish a table, || And if you have anything in your hand, feed me with it.
29.27: Go out, you sojourner, from the face of honor; My brother has come to be my guest; I have need of my house.”
29.28: These things are grievous to a man of understanding: The scolding of [the] household, || And the reproaching of the money-lender.

30.1: He that loves his son will continue to lay stripes on him, || That he may rejoice in him in the end.
30.2: He that disciplines his son will profit from him || And will boast of him among his acquaintance.
30.3: He that teaches his son will provoke his enemy to jealousy; And he will rejoice in him before friends.
30.4: His father dies and is as though he had not died; For he has left one behind him like himself.
30.5: In his life, he saw and rejoiced [in him]; And when he died, he did not sorrow:
30.6: He left behind him an avenger against his enemies, || And one to repay kindness to his friends.
30.7: He that makes too much of his son will bind up his wounds; And his heart will be troubled at every cry.
30.8: An unbroken horse becomes stubborn; And a son left at large becomes headstrong.
30.9: Pamper your child, and he will make you afraid: Play with him, and he will grieve you.
30.10: Do not laugh with him, lest you have sorrow with him; And you will gnash your teeth in the end.
30.11: Give him no liberty in his youth, || And do not wink at his follies.
30.12: Bow his neck down in his youth || And beat him on the sides while he is a child, || Lest he wax stubborn, and be disobedient to you; And there will be sorrow to your soul.
30.13: Discipline your son, and take pains with him, || Lest his shameless behavior be an offense to you.
30.14: Better is a poor man, being sound and strong of constitution, || Than a rich man that is plagued in his body.
30.15: Health and a good constitution are better than all gold; And a strong body than wealth without measure.
30.16: There is no riches better than health of body; And there is no gladness above the joy of the heart.
30.17: Death is better than a bitter life, || And perpetual rest than a continual sickness.
30.18: Good things poured out on a mouth that is closed || Are [as] messes of meat laid on a grave.
30.19: What does an offering profit an idol? For neither will it eat nor smell; So is he that is afflicted of the Lord,
30.20: Seeing with his eyes and groaning, || As a eunuch embracing a virgin and groaning.
30.21: Do not give your soul over to sorrow; And do not afflict yourself in your own counsel.
30.22: Gladness of heart is the life of a man; And the joyfulness of a man is length of days.
30.23: Love your own soul and comfort your heart: And remove sorrow far from you; For sorrow has destroyed many, || And there is no profit therein.
30.24: Envy and wrath shorten [a man’s] days; And care brings old age before the time.
30.25: A cheerful and good heart || Will have a care of his meat and diet.

31.1: Wakefulness that comes of riches consumes the flesh, || And the anxiety thereof puts away sleep.
31.2: Wakeful anxiety will crave slumber; And in a severe disease sleep will be broken.
31.3: A rich man toils in gathering money together; And when he rests, he is filled with his good things.
31.4: A poor man toils in lack of substance; And when he rests, he becomes needy.
31.5: He that loves gold will not be justified; And he that follows destruction will himself have his fill [of it].
31.6: Many have been given over to ruin for the sake of gold; And their perdition meets them face to face.
31.7: It is a stumbling block to those who sacrifice to it; And every fool will be taken with that.
31.8: Blessed is the rich who is found without blemish, || And who does not go after gold.
31.9: Who is he? A nd we will call him blessed; For he has done wonderful things among his people.
31.10: Who has been tried by it, and found perfect? Then let him glory. Who has had the power to transgress, and has not transgressed? And to do evil, and has not done it?
31.11: His goods will be made sure, || And the congregation will declare his alms.
31.12: Do you sit at a great table? Do not be greedy on it, || And do not say, “There are many things on it.”
31.13: Remember that an evil eye is a wicked thing: What has been created more evil than an eye? Therefore, it sheds tears from every face.
31.14: Do not stretch your hand wherever it looks || And do not thrust yourself with it into the dish.
31.15: Consider your neighbor’s [liking] by your own; And be discreet in every point.
31.16: Eat, as [becomes] a man, || Those things which are set before you; And do not eat greedily, lest you be hated.
31.17: Be first to leave off for manners’ sake; And do not be insatiable, lest you offend.
31.18: And if you sit among many, || Do not reach out your hand before them.
31.19: How sufficient to a well-mannered man is a very little, || And he does not breathe hard on his bed.
31.20: Healthy sleep comes of moderate eating; He rises early, and his wits are with him: The pain of wakefulness, and colic, and griping, || Are with an insatiable man.
31.21: And if you have been forced to eat, || Rise up in the midst thereof, and you will have rest.
31.22: Hear me, my son, and do not despise me, || And at the end you will find my words [true]: In all your works be quick, || And no disease will come to you.
31.23: Him that is liberal of his meat the lips will bless; And the testimony of his excellence will be believed.
31.24: The city will murmur at he who is stingy with his meat; And the testimony of his stinginess will be sure.
31.25: Do not show yourself valiant in wine, || For wine has destroyed many.
31.26: The furnace proves the temper [of steel] by dipping; So does wine [prove] hearts in the quarreling of the proud.
31.27: Wine is as good as life to men, || If you drink it in its measure: What life is there to a man that is without wine? And it has been created to make men glad.
31.28: Wine drunk in season, to satisfy, || Is joy of heart and gladness of soul:
31.29: Wine drunk abundantly is bitterness of soul, || With provocation and conflict.
31.30: Drunkenness increases the rage of a fool to his hurt; It diminishes strength and adds wounds.
31.31: Do not rebuke your neighbor at a banquet of wine, || Neither set him at nothing in his mirth; Do not speak a word of reproach to him, || And do not press on him by asking back [a debt].

32.1: Have they made you ruler [of a feast]? Do not be lifted up; Be among them as one of them; Take thought for them, and so sit down.
32.2: And when you have done all your office, take your place, || That you may be gladdened on their account, || And receive a crown for your well ordering.
32.3: Speak, you that are the elder, || For it is suitable for you, [but] with sound knowledge; And do not hinder music.
32.4: Do not pour out talk where there is a performance of music, || And do not display your wisdom out of season.
32.5: [As] a signet of carbuncle in a setting of gold, || [So] is a concert of music in a banquet of wine.
32.6: [As] a signet of emerald in a work of gold, || [So] is a strain of music with pleasant wine.
32.7: Speak, young man, if there is need of you; [Yet] scarcely if you be twice asked.
32.8: Sum up your speech, many things in few words; Be as one that knows and yet holds his tongue.
32.9: [If you be] among great men, || Do not behave as their equal; And when another is speaking, || Do not make much babbling.
32.10: Lightning speeds before thunder; And before a modest man favor will go out.
32.11: Rise up on time and do not be the last; Get home quickly and do not loiter:
32.12: Take your pastime there and do what is in your heart; And do not sin by proud speech:
32.13: And for these things bless Him that made you || And gives you to drink freely of His good things.
32.14: He that fears the Lord will receive [His] discipline; And those who seek [Him] early will find favor.
32.15: He that seeks the Law will be filled with that, || But the hypocrite will stumble at that.
32.16: They that fear the Lord will find judgment || And will kindle righteous acts as a light.
32.17: A sinful man shuns reproof || And will find a judgment according to his will.
32.18: A man of counsel will not neglect a thought; A strange and proud man will not crouch in fear, || Even after he has done a thing by himself without counsel.
32.19: Do nothing without counsel; And when you have once done [it], || Do not convert [from it].
32.20: Do not go in a way of conflict; And do not stumble in stony places.
32.21: Do not be confident in a smooth way.
32.22: And beware of your own children.
32.23: In every work trust your own soul; For this is the keeping of the commands.
32.24: He that believes the Law gives heed to the command; And he that trusts in the Lord will suffer no loss.

33.1: No evil will happen to him that fears the Lord, || But in temptation He will once again deliver him.
33.2: A wise man will not hate the Law; But he that is a hypocrite therein is as a ship in a storm.
33.3: A man of understanding will put his trust in the Law; And the Law is faithful to him, || As when one asks at the oracle.
33.4: Prepare [your] speech, and so will you be heard; Bind up instruction and make your answer.
33.5: The heart of a fool is [as] a cartwheel; And his thoughts like a rolling axle-tree.
33.6: A stallion horse is as a mocking friend; He neighs under everyone that sits on him.
33.7: Why does one day excel [over] another, || When all the light of every day in the year is of the sun?
33.8: By the knowledge of the Lord they were distinguished; And He varied seasons and feasts:
33.9: Some of them He exalted and hallowed, || And some of them He has made ordinary days.
33.10: And all men are from the ground, || And Adam was created of earth.
33.11: In the abundance of His knowledge the Lord distinguished them || And made their ways various:
33.12: Some of them He blessed and exalted, || And some of them He hallowed and brought near to Himself; Some of them He cursed and brought low, || And overthrew them from their place.
33.13: As the clay of the potter in his hand, || All his ways are according to his good pleasure; So men are in the hand of Him that made them, || To render to them according to His judgment.
33.14: Good is the opposite of evil, || And life is the opposite of death; So the sinner is the opposite of the godly.
33.15: And so look on all the works of the Most High: Two and two, one against another.
33.16: And I awoke last, as one that gleans after the grape gatherers: By the blessing of the Lord I got before them, || And filled my winepress as one that gathers grapes.
33.17: Consider that I did not labor for myself alone, || But for all those who seek instruction.
33.18: Hear me, you great men of the people, || And listen with your ears, you rulers of the congregation.
33.19: To son and wife, to brother and friend, || Do not give power over yourself while you live; And do not give your goods to another, || Lest you convert and make supplication for them [again].
33.20: While you yet live, and breath is in you, || Do not give yourself over to anybody.
33.21: For it is better that your children should supplicate you, || Than that you should look to the hand of your sons.
33.22: In all your works keep the upper hand; Do not bring a stain on your honor.
33.23: In the day that you end the days of your life, || And in the time of death, distribute your inheritance.
33.24: Fodder, a stick, and burdens, for a donkey; Bread, and discipline, and work, for a servant.
33.25: Set your servant to work, and you will find rest: Leave his hands idle, and he will seek liberty.
33.26: Yoke and thong will bow the neck: And for an evil servant there are racks and tortures.
33.27: Send him to labor, that he may not be idle; For idleness teaches much mischief.
33.28: Set him to work, as is fit for him; And if he does not obey, make his chains heavy.
33.29: And do not be excessive toward any; And do nothing without judgment.
33.30: If you have a servant, let him be as yourself, || Because you have bought him with blood.
33.31: If you have a servant, treat him as yourself, || For you will have need of him as your own soul: If you treat him ill, and he departs and runs away, || Which way will you go to seek him?

34.1: Vain and false hopes are for a man void of understanding; And dreams give wings to fools.
34.2: As one that catches at a shadow, || And follows after the wind, || So is he that sets his mind on dreams.
34.3: The vision of dreams is [as] this thing against that, || The likeness of a face near a face.
34.4: Of an unclean thing what will be cleansed? And of that which is false what will be true?
34.5: Divinations, and soothsayings, and dreams, are vain: And the heart has fantasies, as a woman’s in travail.
34.6: If they are not sent from the Most High in [your] visitation, || Do not give your heart to them.
34.7: For dreams have led many astray: And they have failed by putting their hope in them.
34.8: Without lying the Law will be accomplished; And wisdom is perfection to a faithful mouth.
34.9: A well-instructed man knows many things; And he that has much experience will declare understanding.
34.10: He that has no experience knows few things, || But he that has wandered will increase [his] skill.
34.11: In my wandering I have seen many things; And my understanding is more than my words.
34.12: I was often in danger even to death; And I was preserved because of these things.
34.13: The spirit of those that fear the Lord will live; For their hope is on Him that saves them.
34.14: Whoever fears the Lord will not be afraid || And will not play the coward; For He is his hope.
34.15: Blessed is the soul of him that fears the Lord: To whom does he give heed? A nd who is his stay?
34.16: The eyes of the Lord are on those who love Him, || A mighty protection and strong stay, || A cover from the hot blast, and a cover from the noonday, || A guard from stumbling, and a help from falling.
34.17: He raises up the soul, and enlightens the eyes: He gives healing, life, and blessing.
34.18: He that sacrifices of a thing wrongfully gotten—His offering is made in mockery; And the mockeries of wicked men are not well-pleasing.
34.19: The Most High has no pleasure in the offerings of the ungodly; Neither is He pacified for sins by the multitude of sacrifices.
34.20: [As] one that kills the son before his father’s eyes || Is he that brings a sacrifice from the goods of the poor.
34.21: The bread of the needy is the life of the poor: He that deprives him thereof is a man of blood.
34.22: [As] one that slays his neighbor || Is he that takes away his living; And [as] a shedder of blood || Is he that deprives a hireling of his hire.
34.23: One building, and another pulling down—What profit have they had but toil?
34.24: One praying, and another cursing—Whose voice will the Lord listen to?
34.25: He that washes himself after [touching] a dead body, and touches it again—What profit does he have in his washing?
34.26: Even so a man fasting for his sins, and going again, and doing the same—Who will listen to his prayer? And what profit does he have in his humiliation?

35.1: He that keeps the Law multiplies offerings; He that takes heed to the commands sacrifices a peace offering.
35.2: He that returns a favor offers fine flour; And he that gives alms sacrifices a thank offering.
35.3: To depart from wickedness is a thing pleasing to the Lord; And to depart from unrighteousness is an atoning sacrifice.
35.4: See that you do not appear in the presence of the Lord empty.
35.5: For all these things [are to be done] because of the command.
35.6: The offering of the righteous makes the altar fat; And the sweet savor thereof [is] before the Most High.
35.7: The sacrifice of a righteous man is acceptable; And the memorial thereof will not be forgotten.
35.8: Glorify the Lord with a good eye, || And do not limit the first- fruits of your hands.
35.9: In every gift show a cheerful countenance || And dedicate your tithe with gladness.
35.10: Give to the Most High according as He has given; And as your hand has found, [give] with a good eye.
35.11: For the Lord rewards, || And He will reward you sevenfold.
35.12: Do not think to corrupt with gifts, || For He will not receive them: And do not set your mind on an unrighteous sacrifice, || For the Lord is judge, || And with Him is no respect of persons.
35.13: He will not accept any person against a poor man; And He will listen to the prayer of him that is wronged.
35.14: He will in no way despise the supplication of the fatherless; Nor the widow, when she pours out her tale.
35.15: Do the tears of the widow not run down her cheek? And is her cry not against him that has caused them to fall?
35.16: He that serves [God] according to His good pleasure will be accepted, || And his supplication will reach to the clouds.
35.17: The prayer of the humble pierces the clouds; And until it comes near, he will not be comforted; And he will not depart, until the Most High will visit; And He will judge righteously and execute judgment.
35.18: And the Lord will not be slack, || Neither will He be patient toward them, || Until He has crushed the loins of the unmerciful; And He will repay vengeance to the heathen, || Until He has taken away the multitude of the arrogant, || And broken in pieces the scepters of the unrighteous;
35.19: Until He has rendered to [every] man according to his doings, || And [to] the works of men according to their plans; Until He has judged the cause of His people; And He will make them to rejoice in His mercy.
35.20: Mercy is seasonable in the time of His afflicting [them], || As clouds of rain in the time of drought.

36.1: Have mercy on us, O Lord the God of all, and behold;
36.2: And send Your fear on all the nations:
36.3: Lift up Your hand against the strange nations; And let them see Your mighty power.
36.4: As You were sanctified in us before them, || So be magnified in them before us.
36.5: And let them know You, as we also have known You, || That there is no God but only You, O God.
36.6: Show new signs and work various wonders; Glorify Your hand and Your right arm.
36.7: Raise up indignation, and pour out wrath; Take away the adversary, and destroy the enemy.
36.8: Hasten the time and remember the oath; And let them declare Your mighty works.
36.9: Let him that escapes be devoured by the rage of fire; And may those who harm Your people find destruction.
36.10: Crush the heads of the rulers of the enemies, || That say, “There is none but us.”
36.11: Gather all the tribes of Jacob together, || And take them for Your inheritance, as from the beginning.
36.12: O Lord, have mercy on the people that is called by Your Name, || And on Israel, whom You did liken to a firstborn.
36.13: Have compassion on the city of Your sanctuary: Jerusalem, the place of Your rest.
36.14: Fill Zion; exalt Your oracles || And [fill] Your people with Your glory.
36.15: Give testimony to those that were Your creatures in the beginning || And raise up the prophecies that have been in Your Name.
36.16: Give reward to those who wait for You: And men will put their trust in Your prophets.
36.17: Listen, O Lord, to the prayer of Your suppliants, || According to the blessing of Aaron concerning Your people; And all those who are on the earth will know || That You are the Lord, the perpetual God.
36.18: The belly will eat any meat; Yet one meat is better than another.
36.19: The mouth tastes meats taken in hunting; So does an understanding heart false speeches.
36.20: A contrary heart will cause heaviness: And a man of experience will reward him.
36.21: A woman will receive any man; But one daughter is better than another.
36.22: The beauty of a woman cheers the countenance; And a man desires nothing so much.
36.23: If there is mercy and meekness on her tongue, || Her husband is not like the sons of men.
36.24: He that gets a wife enters on a possession: A help appropriate for him, and a pillar of rest.
36.25: Where no hedge is, the possession will be laid waste; And he that has no wife will mourn as he wanders up and down.
36.26: For who will trust a nimble robber || Who skips from city to city? Even so, [who will trust] a man that has no nest, || And [who] lodges wherever he finds himself at nightfall?

37.1: Every friend will say, “I am also his friend,” || But there is a friend who is only a friend in name.
37.2: Is there not a grief in it even to death, || When a companion and friend is turned to enmity?
37.3: O wicked imagination, from where did you come, || Rolling in to cover the dry land with deceitfulness?
37.4: There is a companion who rejoices in the gladness of a friend, || But in time of affliction will be against him.
37.5: There is a companion who for the belly’s sake labors with his friend, || [But] in the face of battle will take up the buckler.
37.6: Do not forget a friend in your soul; And do not be unmindful of him in your riches.
37.7: Every counselor extols counsel; But there is [one] who counsels for himself.
37.8: Let your soul beware of a counselor || And know beforehand what his interest is, || For he will take counsel for himself; Lest he casts the lot on you,
37.9: And says to you, “Your way is good”: And he will stand near you, || To see what will happen to you.
37.10: Take no counsel with one that looks suspiciously at you; And hide your counsel from such as are jealous of you.
37.11: [Take no counsel] with a woman about her rival; Neither with a coward about war; Nor with a merchant about exchange; Nor with a buyer about selling; Nor with an envious man about thankfulness; Nor with an unmerciful man about kindness; Nor with a sluggard about any kind of work; Nor with a hireling in your house about finishing [his work]; Nor with an idle servant about much business: Give no heed to these in any matter of counsel,
37.12: But rather be continually with a godly man, || Whom you will have known to be a keeper of the commands, || Who in his soul is as your own soul, || And who will grieve with you, if you will miscarry.
37.13: And make the counsel of your heart to stand; For there is none more faithful to you than it.
37.14: For a man’s soul is sometimes accustomed to bring him tidings—More than seven watchmen that sit up high on a watchtower.
37.15: And above all this, entreat the Most High, || That He may direct your way in truth.
37.16: Let reason be the beginning of every work, || And let counsel go before every action.
37.17: As a token of the changing of the heart,
37.18: Four manner of things rise up: Good and evil, life and death; And that which rules over them continually is the tongue.
37.19: There is one that is shrewd [and] the instructor of many, || And yet is unprofitable to his own soul.
37.20: There is [one] that is subtle in words and is hated; He will be destitute of all food:
37.21: For grace was not given to him from the Lord, || Because he is deprived of all wisdom.
37.22: There is one that is wise to his own soul, || And the fruits of his understanding are trustworthy in the mouth.
37.23: A wise man will instruct his own people, || And the fruits of his understanding are trustworthy.
37.24: A wise man will be filled with blessing, || And all those who see him will call him happy.
37.25: The life of man is counted by days, || And the days of Israel are innumerable.
37.26: The wise man will inherit confidence among his people, || And his name will live forever.
37.27: My son, prove your soul in your life, || And see what is evil for it, and do not give that to it.
37.28: For all things are not profitable for all men, || Neither does every soul have pleasure in everything.
37.29: Do not be insatiable in any luxury, || And do not be greedy in the things that you eat.
37.30: For in multitude of meats there will be disease, || And gorging will come near to colic.
37.31: Because of gorging many have perished; But he that takes heed will prolong his life.

38.1: Honor a physician according to your need [of him] || With the honors due to him; For the Lord has truly created him.
38.2: For from the Most High comes healing; And from the king he will receive a gift.
38.3: The skill of the physician will lift up his head; And in the sight of great men he will be admired.
38.4: The Lord created medicines out of the earth; And a prudent man will have no disgust at them.
38.5: Was water not made sweet with wood, || That the virtue thereof might be known?
38.6: And He gave men skill, || That they might be glorified in His marvelous works.
38.7: With them He heals [a man and] takes away his pain.
38.8: With these the apothecary will make a confection; And his works will not be brought to an end; And from him is peace on the face of the earth.
38.9: My son, in your sickness do not be negligent; But pray to the Lord, and He will heal you.
38.10: Put away wrongdoing, || And order your hands correctly, || And cleanse your heart from all manner of sin.
38.11: Give a sweet savor, and a memorial of fine flour; And make fat your offering, as one that is not.
38.12: Then give place to the physician, || For the Lord has truly created him; And do not let him go from you, for you have need of him.
38.13: There is a time when in their very hands is the issue for good.
38.14: For they will also implore the Lord, || That He may prosper them in [giving] relief and in healing for the maintenance of life.
38.15: He that sins before his Maker, || Let him fall into the hands of the physician.
38.16: My son, let your tears fall over the dead, || And as one that suffers grievously begin lamentation; And wind up his body according to his due, || And do not neglect his burial.
38.17: Make bitter weeping, and make passionate wailing, || And let your mourning be according to his merit—For one day or two, || Lest you be spoken evil of: And so be comforted for your sorrow.
38.18: For of sorrow comes death, || And sorrow of heart will bow down the strength.
38.19: In calamity sorrow also remains: And the poor man’s life is grievous to the heart.
38.20: Do not give your heart to sorrow. Put it away, remembering the latter end.
38.21: Do not forget it, || For there is no returning again: You will not profit him, || And you will hurt yourself.
38.22: Remember the sentence on him; For so yours will also be; Yesterday for me, and today for you.
38.23: When the dead is at rest, || Let his remembrance rest; And be comforted for him, || When his spirit departs from him.
38.24: The wisdom of the scribe comes by opportunity of leisure; And he that has little business will become wise.
38.25: How will he become wise that holds the plow, || That glories in the shaft of the goad, || That drives oxen, and is occupied in their labors, || And whose discourse is of the stock of bulls?
38.26: He will set his heart on turning his furrows; And his wakefulness is to give his heifers their fodder.
38.27: So is every craftsman and master workman, || That passes his time by night as by day; They that cut gravings of signets, || And his diligence is to make great variety; He will set his heart to preserve likeness in his portraiture || And will be wakeful to finish his work.
38.28: So is the smith sitting by the anvil and considering the unworked iron: The vapor of the fire will waste his flesh; And in the heat of the furnace he will wrestle [with his work]: The noise of the hammer will be ever in his ear, || And his eyes are on the pattern of the vessel; He will set his heart on perfecting his works, || And he will be wakeful to adorn them perfectly.
38.29: So is the potter sitting at his work, || And turning the wheel around with his feet, || Who is always anxiously set at his work, || And all his handiwork is by number;
38.30: He will fashion the clay with his arm || And will bend its strength in front of his feet; He will apply his heart to finish the glazing; And he will be wakeful to make clean the furnace.
38.31: All these put their trust in their hands; And each becomes wise in his own work.
38.32: Without these a city will not be inhabited, || And men will not sojourn nor walk up and down [therein].
38.33: They will not be sought for in the council of the people, || And in the assembly, they will not mount on high; They will not sit on the seat of the judge, || And they will not understand the covenant of judgment: Neither will they declare instruction and judgment; And where allegories are, they will not be found.
38.34: But they will maintain the fabric of the world; And in the handiwork of their craft is their prayer.

39.1: Not so he that has applied his soul || And meditates in the Law of the Most High; He will seek out the wisdom of all the ancients || And will be occupied in prophecies.
39.2: He will keep the discourse of the men of renown || And will enter in amidst the subtleties of allegories.
39.3: He will seek out the hidden meaning of proverbs || And be conversant in the dark sayings of allegories.
39.4: He will serve among great men || And appear before him that rules. He will travel through the land of strange nations; For he has tried good things and evil among men.
39.5: He will apply his heart to return early to the Lord that made him, || And will make supplication before the Most High, || And will open his mouth in prayer, || And will make supplication for his sins.
39.6: If the great Lord wills, || He will be filled with the spirit of understanding: He will pour out the words of his wisdom, || And in prayer give thanks to the Lord.
39.7: He will direct his counsel and knowledge, || And in His secrets he will meditate.
39.8: He will show out the instruction which he has been taught || And will glory in the Law of the covenant of the Lord.
39.9: Many will commend his understanding; And so long as the world endures, || It will not be blotted out: His memorial will not depart, || And his name will live from generation to generation.
39.10: Nations will declare his wisdom, || And the congregation will tell out his praise.
39.11: If he continues, he will leave a greater name than one thousand, || And if he dies, he adds to that.
39.12: Yet more I will utter, which I have thought on; And I am filled as the moon at the full.
39.13: Listen to me, you holy children, || And bud out as a rose growing by a brook of water:
39.14: And give a sweet savor as frankincense, || And put out flowers as a lily; Spread abroad a sweet smell, || And sing a song of praise; Bless the Lord for all His works.
39.15: Magnify His Name, and give utterance to His praise || With the songs of your lips, and with harps; And so will you say when you utter [His praise]:
39.16: “All the works of the Lord are exceedingly good, || And every command will be [accomplished] in his season.”
39.17: None can say, “What is this? What is that?” For in his season they will all be sought out. At His word the waters stood as a heap, || And the receptacles of waters at the word of His mouth.
39.18: At His command all His good pleasure is [done]; And there is none that will hinder His salvation.
39.19: The works of all flesh are before Him; And it is not possible to be hid from His eyes.
39.20: He sees from age to age; And there is nothing wonderful before Him.
39.21: None can say, “What is this? What is that?” For all things are created for their uses.
39.22: His blessing covered the dry land as a river || And saturated it as a flood.
39.23: As He has made the waters salty, || So the heathen will inherit His wrath.
39.24: His ways are plain to the holy, || So are they stumbling blocks to the wicked.
39.25: Good things are created from the beginning for the good, || So are evil things for sinners.
39.26: The chief of all things necessary for the life of man || Are water, and fire, and iron, and salt, || And flour of wheat, and honey, and milk, || The blood of the grape, and oil, and clothing.
39.27: All these things are for good to the godly; So to the sinners they will be turned into evil.
39.28: There are winds that are created for vengeance, || And in their fury their scourges lay on heavily; In the time of consummation they pour out their strength || And will appease the wrath of Him that made them.
39.29: Fire, and hail, and famine, and death—All these are created for vengeance—
39.30: Teeth of wild beasts, and scorpions and adders, || And a sword punishing the ungodly to destruction.
39.31: They will rejoice in His command, || And will be made ready on earth when there is need; And in their seasons they will not transgress [His] word.
39.32: Therefore, I was resolved from the beginning, || And I considered [this, and] left it in writing;
39.33: All the works of the Lord are good: And He will supply every need in its season.
39.34: And none can say, “This is worse than that,” || For they will all be well approved in their season.
39.35: And now with all your heart and mouth || Sing praises and bless the Name of the Lord.

40.1: Great travail is created for every man, || And a heavy yoke is on the sons of Adam, || From the day of their coming out from their mother’s womb, || Until the day for their burial in the mother of all things.
40.2: The expectation of things to come, and the day of death, || [Trouble] their thoughts, and [cause] fear of heart—
40.3: From him that sits on a lofty throne, || Even to him that is humbled in earth and ashes;
40.4: From him that wears purple and a crown, || Even to him that is clothed with a hempen gown.
40.5: [There is] wrath, and jealousy, and trouble, and disquiet, || And fear of death, and anger, and strife; And in the time of rest on his bed || His night sleep changes his knowledge.
40.6: Little or nothing is his resting, || And afterward in his sleep, as in a day of keeping watch, || He is troubled in the vision of his heart, || As one that has escaped from the front of battle.
40.7: In the very time of his deliverance he awakens, || And marvels that the fear is nothing.
40.8: [It is so] with all flesh, from man to beast, || And on sinners sevenfold more.
40.9: Death, and bloodshed, and strife, and sword, || Calamities, famine, suffering, and the scourge—
40.10: All these things were created for the wicked, || And because of them the flood came.
40.11: All things that are of the earth return to the earth again, || And [all things that are] of the waters return into the sea.
40.12: All bribery and injustice will be blotted out; And good faith will stand forever.
40.13: The goods of the unjust will be dried up like a river, || And like a great thunder in rain will go off in noise.
40.14: In opening his hands, [a man] will be made glad; So will transgressors utterly fail.
40.15: The children of the ungodly will not put out many branches; And [they are as] unclean roots on a sheer rock.
40.16: The sedge [that grows] on every water and bank of a river || Will be plucked up before all grass.
40.17: Bounty is as a garden of blessings, || And kindness endures forever.
40.18: The life of one that labors, and is content, will be made sweet; And he that finds a treasure is above both.
40.19: Children and the building of a city establish a name; And a blameless wife is counted above both.
40.20: Wine and music make the heart rejoice; And the love of wisdom is above both.
40.21: The pipe and the lute make pleasant melody; And a pleasant tongue is above both.
40.22: Your eye will desire grace and beauty; And above both—the green blade of corn.
40.23: A friend and a companion never meet wrongly; And a wife with her husband is above both.
40.24: Brothers and help are for a time of affliction; And kindness is a deliverer above both.
40.25: Gold and silver will make the foot stand sure; And counsel is esteemed above them both.
40.26: Riches and strength will lift up the heart; And the fear of the Lord is above both: There is nothing lacking in the fear of the Lord, || And there is no need to seek help therein.
40.27: The fear of the Lord is as a garden of blessing || And [it] covers a man above all glory.
40.28: My son, do not lead a beggar’s life; It is better to die than to beg.
40.29: A man that looks to the table of another, || His life is not to be counted for a life; He will pollute his soul with another man’s meats, || But a man wise and well-instructed will beware thereof.
40.30: In the mouth of the shameless, begging will be sweet; And in his belly a fire will be kindled.

41.1: O death, how bitter is the remembrance of you || To a man that is at peace in his possessions, || To the man that has nothing to distract him, || And [who] has prosperity in all things, || And who still has strength to receive meat!
41.2: O death, your sentence is acceptable to a man that is needy, || And that fails in strength, that is in extreme old age, || And is distracted about all things, and is perverse, and has lost patience!
41.3: Do not be afraid of the sentence of death; Remember those who have been before you, || And that come after: This is the sentence from the Lord over all flesh.
41.4: And why do you refuse, || When it is the good pleasure of the Most High? Whether it be ten, or one hundred, or one thousand years, || There is no inquisition of life in the grave.
41.5: The children of sinners are abominable children, || And they frequent the dwellings of the ungodly.
41.6: The inheritance of sinners’ children will perish, || And a perpetual reproach will be with their posterity.
41.7: Children will complain of an ungodly father, || Because they will be reproached for his sake.
41.8: Woe to you, ungodly men, || Which have forsaken the Law of the Most High God!
41.9: If you are born, you will be born to a curse; If you die, a curse will be your portion.
41.10: All things that are of the earth will go back to the earth; So the ungodly will go from a curse to perdition.
41.11: The mourning of men is about their bodies, || But the name of sinners being evil will be blotted out.
41.12: Have regard for your name, || For it continues with you longer than one thousand great treasures of gold.
41.13: A good life has its number of days; And a good name continues forever.
41.14: My children, keep instruction in peace, || But wisdom that is hid, and a treasure that is not seen, || What profit is in them both?
41.15: Better is a man that hides his foolishness || Than a man that hides his wisdom.
41.16: Why show reverence to my word, || For it is not good to retain every kind of shame; And not all things are approved by all in good faith.
