41.17: Be ashamed of whoredom before father and mother, || And of a lie before a prince and a mighty man;
41.18: Of an offense before a judge and ruler; Of iniquity before the congregation and the people; Of unjust dealing before a partner and friend;
41.19: And of theft in regard of the place where you sojourn, || And in regard of the truth of God and His covenant; And of leaning with your elbow at meat; And of vulgarity in the matter of giving and taking;
41.20: And of silence before those who salute you; And of looking on a woman that is a prostitute;
41.21: And of turning your face away from a relative; Of taking away a portion or a gift; And of gazing on a woman that has a husband;
41.22: Of being over-busy with his maid—And do not come near her bed; Of scolding speeches before friends; And after you have given, do not scold;
41.23: Of repeating and speaking what you have heard; And of revealing of secrets.
41.24: So you will be truly modest and find favor in the sight of every man.

42.1: Do not be ashamed of these things, || And accept no man’s person to sin [by it]:
42.2: Of the Law of the Most High, and His covenant; And of judgment to do justice to the ungodly;
42.3: Of reckoning with a partner and with travelers; And of a gift from the heritage of friends;
42.4: Of exactness of balance and weights; And of getting much or little;
42.5: Of indifferent selling of merchants; And of much correction of children; And of making the side of an evil servant to bleed.
42.6: Keeping a seal is good where an evil wife is; And where many hands are, shut [things] closed.
42.7: Whatever you hand over, || Let it be by number and weight; And in giving and receiving, || Let all be in writing.
42.8: [Do not be ashamed] to instruct the unwise and foolish, || And one of extreme old age that contends with those that are young; And so you will be well instructed indeed, || And approved in the sight of every living man.
42.9: A daughter is a secret cause of wakefulness to a father; And the care for her puts away sleep in her youth, || Lest she pass the flower of her age; And when she is married, || Lest she should be hated;
42.10: In her virginity, || Lest she should be defiled || And be with child in her father’s house; And when she has a husband, || Lest she should transgress; And when she is married, || Lest she should be barren.
42.11: Keep a strict watch over a headstrong daughter, || Lest she makes you a laughing-stock to your enemies, || A byword in the city and notorious among the people, || And shame you before the multitude.
42.12: Do not look on everybody in regard of beauty || And do not sit in the midst of women;
42.13: For from garments comes a moth, || And from a woman a woman’s wickedness.
42.14: Better is the wickedness of a man || Than a pleasant-dealing woman, || And a woman which puts you to shameful reproach.
42.15: I will now make mention of the works of the Lord || And will declare the things that I have seen: In the words of the Lord are His works.
42.16: The sun that gives light looks on all things; And the work of the Lord is full of His glory.
42.17: The Lord has not given power to the saints || To declare all His marvelous works, || Which the Almighty Lord firmly settled, || That whatever exists might be established in His glory.
42.18: He searches out the deep, and the heart, || And He has understanding of their cunning plans; For the Most High knows all knowledge, || And He looks into the signs of the world,
42.19: Declaring the things that are past, || And the things that will be, || And revealing the traces of hidden things.
42.20: No thought escapes Him; There is no word hidden from Him.
42.21: He has ordered the mighty works of His wisdom—[He] who is from age to age: Nothing has been added to them, || Nor diminished from them; And He had no need of any counselor.
42.22: How desirable are all His works! One may behold [this] even to a spark.
42.23: All these things live and remain forever in all manner of uses, || And they are all obedient.
42.24: All things are double—one against another: And He has made nothing imperfect.
42.25: One thing establishes the good things of another; And who will be filled with beholding His glory?

43.1: The pride of the height is the expanse in its clearness, || The appearance of Heaven, in the spectacle of its glory.
43.2: The sun when he appears, || Bringing tidings as he goes out, || Is a marvelous instrument, the work of the Most High:
43.3: At his noon he dries up the country, || And who will stand against his burning heat?
43.4: A man blowing a furnace is in works of heat, || [But] the sun [is] three times more: Burning up the mountains, || Breathing out fiery vapors, || And sending out bright beams—he dims the eyes.
43.5: Great is the Lord that made him; And at His word he hastens his course.
43.6: The moon is also in all things for her season, || For a declaration of times, and a sign of the world.
43.7: From the moon is the sign of the feast day—A light that wanes when she has come to the full.
43.8: The month is called after her name, || Increasing wonderfully in her changing; An instrument of the army on high, || Shining out in the expanse of Heaven;
43.9: The beauty of Heaven, the glory of the stars, || An ornament giving light in the highest places of the Lord.
43.10: At the word of the Holy One they will stand in due order, || And they will not faint in their watches.
43.11: Look on the rainbow and praise Him that made it—Exceedingly beautiful in the brightness thereof.
43.12: It encircles the Heaven around with a circle of glory; The hands of the Most High have stretched it.
43.13: By His command He makes the snow to fall speedily || And swiftly sends the lightnings of His judgment.
43.14: By reason thereof the treasure-houses are opened; And clouds fly out as birds.
43.15: By His mighty power He makes the clouds strong, || And the hailstones are broken small:
43.16: And at His appearing the mountains will be shaken, || And at His will the south wind will blow.
43.17: The voice of His thunder makes the earth to travail; So does the northern storm and the whirlwind. As birds flying down, He sprinkles the snow; And as the lighting of the locust is the falling down thereof:
43.18: The eye will marvel at the beauty of its whiteness, || And the heart will be astonished at the raining of it.
43.19: He also pours the hoarfrost on the earth as salt; And when it is congealed, it is [as] points of thorns.
43.20: The cold north wind will blow, || And the ice will be congealed on the water: It will lodge on every gathering of water, || And the water will put it on as [if] it were a breastplate.
43.21: It will devour the mountains, and burn up the wilderness, || And consume the green herb as fire.
43.22: A mist coming speedily is the healing of all things; A dew coming after heat will bring cheerfulness.
43.23: By His counsel He has stilled the deep, || And planted islands therein.
43.24: They that sail on the sea tell of the danger thereof; And when we hear it with our ears, we marvel.
43.25: Therein are also those strange and wondrous works, || A variety of all that has life—the race of sea- monsters.
43.26: By reason of Him his end has success, || And by His word all things consist.
43.27: We may say many things, || Yet we will not attain [to them]; And the sum of our words is, “He is all.”
43.28: How will we have strength to glorify Him? For He Himself is the great one above all His works.
43.29: The Lord is terrible and exceedingly great; And His power is marvelous.
43.30: When you glorify the Lord, || Exalt Him as much as you can; For even yet He will exceed: And when you exalt Him, put out your full strength: Do not be weary, for you will never attain.
43.31: Who has seen Him, that he may declare Him? And who will magnify Him as He is?
43.32: Many things are hidden greater than these; For we have seen but a few of His works.
43.33: For the Lord made all things; And He gave wisdom to the godly.

44.1: Let us now praise famous men, || And our fathers that became the father of us.
44.2: The Lord manifested [in them] great glory, || [Even] His mighty power from the beginning:
44.3: Such as bore rule in their kingdoms, || And were men renowned for their power, || Giving counsel by their understanding; Such as have brought tidings in prophecies;
44.4: Leaders of the people by their counsels, || And by their understanding [men of] learning for the people—Wise [were] their words in their instruction;
44.5: Such as sought out musical tunes, || And set out verses in writing;
44.6: Rich men furnished with ability, || Living peaceably in their habitations:
44.7: All these were honored in their generations, || And were a glory in their days.
44.8: There is of them, that have left a name behind them, || To declare their praises.
44.9: And there are some which have no memorial, || Who have perished as though they had not been || And have become as though they had not been born— And their children after them.
44.10: But these were men of mercy, || Whose righteous deeds have not been forgotten.
44.11: A good inheritance will remain with their seed continually; Their children [are] within the covenants.
44.12: Their seed stands fast, || And their children for their sakes.
44.13: Their seed will remain forever, || And their glory will not be blotted out.
44.14: Their bodies were buried in peace, || And their name lives to all generations.
44.15: Peoples will declare their wisdom, || And the congregation tells out their praise.
44.16: Enoch pleased the Lord, and was translated, || [Being] an example of conversion to all generations.
44.17: Noah was found perfect [and] righteous; In the season of wrath, he was taken in exchange [for the world]; Therefore a remnant was left to the earth when the flood came.
44.18: Perpetual covenants were made with him, || That all flesh should no longer be blotted out by a flood.
44.19: Abraham was a great father of a multitude of nations; And there was none found like him in glory,
44.20: Who kept the Law of the Most High || And was taken into covenant with Him: In his flesh he established the covenant; And when he was tested, he was found faithful.
44.21: Therefore He assured him by an oath, || That the nations should be blessed in his seed; That He would multiply him as the dust of the earth, || And exalt his seed as the stars, || And cause them to inherit from sea to sea, || And from the River to the utmost part of the earth.
44.22: In Isaac He also established likewise, for his father Abraham’s sake, || The blessing of all men, and the covenant:
44.23: And He made it rest on the head of Jacob; He acknowledged him in His blessings, || And gave to him by inheritance, and divided his portions; He parted them among [the] twelve tribes.

45.1: And He brought out of him a man of mercy, || Which found favor in the sight of all flesh; A man beloved of God and men—Even Moses, whose memorial is blessed.
45.2: He made him like to the glory of the saints || And magnified him in the fears of his enemies.
45.3: By his words He caused the wonders to cease; He glorified him in the sight of kings; He gave him command for his people || And showed him part of His glory.
45.4: He sanctified him in his faithfulness and meekness; He chose him out of all flesh.
45.5: He made him to hear His voice, || And led him into the thick darkness, || And gave him commands face to face—Even the law of life and knowledge, || That he might teach Jacob the covenant, || And Israel His judgments.
45.6: He exalted Aaron, a holy man like to him, || Even his brother, of the tribe of Levi.
45.7: He established a perpetual covenant for him || And gave him the priesthood of the people; He beautified him with attractive ornaments || And girded him around with a robe of glory.
45.8: He clothed him with the perfection of exultation, || And strengthened him with apparel of honor: The linen trousers, the long robe, and the ephod.
45.9: And He compassed him with pomegranates of gold, || And with many bells around —To send out a sound as he went, || To make a sound that might be heard in the temple, || For a memorial to the children of his people—
45.10: With a holy garment, || With gold, and blue, and purple, the work of the embroiderer, || With an oracle of judgment, || With the Lights and Perfections;
45.11: With twisted scarlet, the work of the craftsman, || With precious stones graven like a signet, || In a setting of gold, the work of the jeweler, || For a memorial engraved in writing, || After the number of the tribes of Israel;
45.12: With a crown of gold on the miter, || Having graven on it, as on a signet, Holiness, || An ornament of honor, a work of might, || The desires of the eyes, attractive and beautiful.
45.13: There have never been any such before him; No stranger put them on, || But his sons only, and his offspring perpetually.
45.14: His sacrifices will be wholly consumed every day—twice continually.
45.15: Moses consecrated him and anointed him with holy oil: It was to him for a perpetual covenant, || And to his seed, all the days of Heaven, || To minister to Him, and also to execute the priest’s office, || And to bless His people in His Name.
45.16: He chose him out of all living to offer sacrifice to the Lord, || Incense, and a sweet savor, for a memorial, || To make reconciliation for Your people.
45.17: He gave His commands to him— Authority in the covenants of judgments, || To teach Jacob the testimonies, || And to enlighten Israel in His law.
45.18: Strangers gathered themselves together against him, || And envied him in the wilderness, || [Even] Dathan and Abiram with their company, || And the congregation of Korah, with wrath and anger.
45.19: The Lord saw it, and it displeased Him; And in the wrath of His anger they were destroyed: He did wonders on them, || To consume them with flaming fire.
45.20: And He added glory to Aaron and gave him a heritage: He divided to him the first-fruits of the increase; [And] first he prepared bread in abundance:
45.21: For they will eat the sacrifices of the Lord, || Which He gave to him and to his seed.
45.22: However, in the land of the people he will have no inheritance, || And he has no portion among the people; For He Himself is your portion [and] inheritance.
45.23: And Phinehas the son of Eleazar is the third in glory, || In that he was zealous in the fear of the Lord, || And stood fast in the good forwardness of his soul when the people turned away, || And he made reconciliation for Israel.
45.24: Therefore there was a covenant of peace established for him, || [That he should be] leader of the saints and of His people; That he and his seed should have the dignity of the priesthood forever.
45.25: Also [He made] a covenant with David the son of Jesse, of the tribe of Judah; The inheritance of the king is his alone from son to son; So the inheritance of Aaron is also to his seed.
45.26: [God] give you wisdom in your heart to judge His people in righteousness, || That their good things may not be abolished, || And [that] their glory [may endure] for all their generations.

46.1: Joshua the son of Nun was valiant in war || And was the successor of Moses in prophecies: Who according to his name was made great for the saving of God’s chosen ones, || To take vengeance of the enemies that rose up against them, || That he might give Israel their inheritance.
46.2: How he was glorified in lifting up his hands, || And in stretching out his sword against the cities!
46.3: Who before him so stood fast? For the Lord Himself brought his enemies to him.
46.4: Did the sun not go back by his hand? And did one day not become as two?
46.5: He called on the Most High [and] Mighty One, || When his enemies pressed him around; And the great Lord heard him.
46.6: With hailstones of mighty power || He caused war to break violently on the nation, || And in the going down he destroyed those who resisted, || That the nations might know his armor, || How that he fought in the sight of the Lord; For he followed after the Mighty One.
46.7: Also in the time of Moses he did a work of mercy—He and Caleb the son of Jephunneh, || In that they withstood the adversary, || Hindered the people from sin, || And stilled the murmuring of wickedness.
46.8: And of six hundred thousand people on foot, || They two alone were preserved to bring them into the heritage, || Even into a land flowing with milk and honey.
46.9: Also the Lord gave strength to Caleb, || And it remained with him to his old age, || So that he entered on the height of the land, || And his seed obtained it for a heritage:
46.10: That all the sons of Israel might see || That it is good to walk after the Lord.
46.11: Also the judges, everyone by His Name—All whose hearts did not go whoring, || And who did not turn away from the Lord —May their memorial be blessed,
46.12: May their bones again flourish out of their place, || And may the name of those who have been honored be renewed on their children.
46.13: Samuel, the prophet of the Lord, beloved of his Lord, || Established a kingdom, and anointed princes over His people.
46.14: By the Law of the L ord he judged the congregation, || And the Lord visited Jacob.
46.15: By his faithfulness he was proved to be a prophet, || And by his words he was known to be faithful in vision.
46.16: Also when his enemies pressed around him, || He called on the Lord, the Mighty One, || With the offering of the sucking lamb.
46.17: And the Lord thundered from Heaven, || And made His voice to be heard with a mighty sound.
46.18: And He utterly destroyed the rulers of the Tyrians, || And all the princes of the Philistines.
46.19: Also before the time of his long sleep || He made protestations in the sight of the Lord and [His] anointed: “I have not taken any man’s goods, so much as a sandal”: And no man did accuse him.
46.20: And after he fell asleep, he prophesied, || And showed the king his end, || And lifted up his voice from the earth in prophecy, || To blot out the wickedness of the people.

47.1: And after him, Nathan rose up to prophesy in the days of David.
47.2: As is the fat when it is separated from the peace offering, || So was David [separated] from the sons of Israel.
47.3: He played with lions as with kids, || And with bears as with lambs of the flock.
47.4: In his youth did he not kill a giant, || And take away reproach from the people, || When he lifted up his hand with a sling stone, || And beat down the boasting of Goliath?
47.5: For he called on the Most High Lord; And He gave him strength in his right hand, || To kill a man mighty in war, || To exalt the horn of His people.
47.6: So they glorified him for [his] tens of thousands, || And praised him for the blessings of the Lord, || In that there was given to him a diadem of glory.
47.7: For he destroyed the enemies on every side, || And brought to nothing the Philistines his adversaries, || [And] broke their horn in pieces to this day.
47.8: In every work of his || He gave thanks to the Holy One Most High with words of glory; With his whole heart he sang praise || And loved Him that made him.
47.9: He also set singers before the altar, || So as to make sweet melody by their music.
47.10: He gave beauty to the feasts, || And set in order the seasons to perfection, || While they praised His holy Name, || And the sanctuary sounded from early morning.
47.11: The Lord took away his sins, || And exalted his horn forever, || And gave him a covenant of kings, || And a throne of glory in Israel.
47.12: After him, [his] son rose up—a man of understanding; And for his sake he lived at large.
47.13: Solomon reigned in days of peace; And God gave to him rest around, || That he might set up a house for His Name || And prepare a sanctuary forever.
47.14: How wise you were made in your youth || And filled as a river with understanding!
47.15: Your soul covered the earth, || And you filled it with dark allegories.
47.16: Your name reached to the isles far off; And you were beloved for your peace.
47.17: For your songs, and proverbs, and allegories, || And for your interpretations, the countries marveled at you.
47.18: By the Name of the Lord God, which is called the God of Israel, || You gathered gold as tin and multiplied silver as lead.
47.19: You bowed your loins to women, || And in your body you were brought into subjection.
47.20: You blemished your honor and profaned your seed, || To bring wrath on your children; And I was grieved for your folly,
47.21: So that the sovereignty was divided, || And out of Ephraim a disobedient kingdom ruled.
47.22: But the Lord will never forsake His mercy; And He will not destroy any of His works, || Nor blot out the posterity of His chosen ones; And the seed of him that loved Him He will not take away; And He gave a remnant to Jacob, || And to David a root out of him.
47.23: And [so] rested Solomon with his fathers; And of his seed he left behind him Rehoboam—[Even] the foolishness of the people, || And one that lacked understanding, || Who made the people to revolt by his counsel. Also Jeroboam the son of Nebat, || Who made Israel to sin, and gave to Ephraim a way of sin.
47.24: And their sins were multiplied exceedingly, || To remove them from their land.
47.25: For they sought out all manner of wickedness, || Until vengeance should come on them.

48.1: Also there arose Elijah the prophet as fire, || And his word burned like a torch:
48.2: Who brought a famine on them, || And by his zeal made them few in number.
48.3: By the word of the Lord he shut up the Heaven: Three times he so brought down fire.
48.4: How you were glorified, O Elijah, in your wondrous deeds! And who will [have] glory like to you?
48.5: Who raised up a dead man from death, || And from the place of the dead, by the word of the Most High;
48.6: Who brought down kings to destruction, || And honorable men from their bed;
48.7: Who heard rebuke in Sinai, || And judgments of vengeance in Horeb;
48.8: Who anointed kings for retribution, || And prophets to succeed after him;
48.9: Who was taken up in a tempest of fire, || In a chariot of fiery horses;
48.10: Who was recorded for reproofs in their seasons, || To pacify anger, before it broke out into wrath; To turn the heart of the father to the son, || And to restore the tribes of Jacob.
48.11: Blessed are those who saw you, || And those who have been beautified with love; For we also will surely live.
48.12: [It was] Elijah who was wrapped in a tempest, || And Elisha was filled with his spirit; And in [all] his days he was not moved by [the fear of] any ruler, || And no one brought him into subjection.
48.13: Nothing was too high for him; And when he was laid on sleep his body prophesied.
48.14: As in his life he did wonders, || So in death his works were marvelous.
48.15: For all this the people did not convert, || And they did not depart from their sins, || Until they were carried away as a plunder from their land || And were scattered through all the earth; And the people were left very few in number, || And a ruler [was left] in the house of David.
48.16: Some of them did that which was pleasing [to God], || [And] some multiplied sins.
48.17: Hezekiah fortified his city || And brought in water into the midst of them: He dug the sheer rock with iron || And built up wells for waters.
48.18: In his days Sennacherib came up, || And sent Rabshakeh, and departed; And he lifted up his hand against Zion || And boasted great things in his arrogance.
48.19: Then their hearts and their hands were shaken, || And they were in pain, as women in travail;
48.20: And they called on the Lord, who is merciful, || Spreading out their hands to Him: And the Holy One heard them speedily out of Heaven, || And delivered them by the hand of Isaiah.
48.21: He struck the camp of the Assyrians, || And His messenger utterly destroyed them.
48.22: For Hezekiah did that which was pleasing to the Lord || And was strong in the ways of his father David, || Which Isaiah the prophet commanded, || Who was great and faithful in his vision.
48.23: In his days the sun went backward; And he added life to the king.
48.24: He saw by an excellent spirit what should come to pass at the end; And he comforted those who mourned in Zion.
48.25: He showed the things that should be to the end of time, || And the hidden things before they came.

49.1: The memorial of Josiah is like the composition of incense || Prepared by the work of the apothecary: It will be sweet as honey in every mouth, || And as music at a banquet of wine.
49.2: He behaved himself uprightly in the conversion of the people || And took away the abominations of iniquity.
49.3: He set his heart right toward the Lord; In the days of wicked men, || He made godliness to prevail.
49.4: Except David, and Hezekiah, and Josiah, all committed trespass; For they forsook the Law of the Most High; The kings of Judah failed.
49.5: For they gave their power to others, || And their glory to a strange nation.
49.6: They set the chosen city of the sanctuary on fire, || And made her streets desolate, [as it was written] by the hand of Jeremiah.
49.7: For they mistreated him; And yet he was sanctified in the womb to be a prophet, || To root out, and to afflict, and to destroy; [And] in like manner to build and to plant.
49.8: [It was] Ezekiel who saw the vision of glory, || Which [God] showed him on the chariot of the cherubim.
49.9: For truly he remembered the enemies in storm, || And to do good to those who directed their ways correctly.
49.10: Also of the twelve prophets || May the bones flourish again out of their place. And he comforted Jacob and delivered them by confidence of hope.
49.11: How will we magnify Zerubbabel? And he was as a signet on the right hand:
49.12: So was Jesus the son of Josedek: Who in their days built the house, || And exalted a people holy to the Lord, prepared for continuous glory.
49.13: Also of Nehemiah the memorial is great: Who raised up for us the walls that were fallen, || And set up the gates and bars, and raised up our homes again.
49.14: No man was created on the earth such as was Enoch; For he was taken up from the earth.
49.15: Neither was there a man born like to Joseph, || A governor of his countrymen, a support of the people: Yes, his bones were visited.
49.16: Shem and Seth were glorified among men; And above every living thing in the creation is Adam.

50.1: [It was] Simon, the son of Onias, the great priest, || Who in his life repaired the house, || And in his days strengthened the temple:
50.2: And by him was built from the foundation the height of the double [wall], || The lofty underworks of the enclosure of the temple:
50.3: In his days the cistern of waters was diminished, || The brazen vessel in compass as the sea.
50.4: [It was] he that took thought for his people that they should not fall || And fortified the city against besieging;
50.5: How glorious was he when the people gathered around him || At his coming out of the sanctuary—
50.6: As the morning star in the midst of a cloud, || As the moon at the full;
50.7: As the sun shining out on the temple of the Most High, || And as the rainbow giving light in clouds of glory;
50.8: As the flower of roses in the days of new [fruits], || As lilies at the water spring, || As the shoot of the frankincense tree in the time of summer;
50.9: As fire and incense in the censer, || As a vessel all of beaten gold, || Adorned with all manner of precious stones;
50.10: As an olive tree budding out fruits, || And as a cypress growing high among the clouds.
50.11: When he took up the robe of glory, || And put on the perfection of exultation, || In the ascent of the holy altar, || He made the precinct of the sanctuary glorious.
50.12: And when he received the portions out of the priests’ hands, || Himself also standing by the hearth of the altar, || His countrymen as a garland around him, || He was as a young cedar in Libanus; And as stems of palm trees compassed around him,
50.13: And all the sons of Aaron in their glory, || And the Lord ’s offering in their hands, || Before all the congregation of Israel.
50.14: And finishing the service at the altars, || That he might adorn the offering of the Most High, the Almighty,
50.15: He stretched out his hand to the cup, || And poured out the cup of the grape; He poured out at the foot of the altar || A sweet smelling savor to the Most High, the King of all.
50.16: Then the sons of Aaron shouted, || They sounded the trumpets of beaten work, || They made a great noise to be heard, || For a remembrance before the Most High.
50.17: Then all the people hurried together || And fell down on the earth on their faces to worship their Lord—The Almighty, God Most High.
50.18: The singers also praised Him with their voices; In the whole house sweet melody was made.
50.19: And the people implored the Lord Most High, || In prayer before Him that is merciful, || Until the worship of the Lord should be ended; And so, they accomplished His service.
50.20: Then he went down and lifted up his hands || Over the whole congregation of the sons of Israel, || To give blessing to the Lord with his lips, || And to glory in His Name.
50.21: And he bowed himself down in worship the second time, || To declare the blessing from the Most High.
50.22: And now bless the God of all, who everywhere does great things, || Who exalts our days from the womb, || And deals with us according to His mercy.
50.23: May He grant us joyfulness of heart, || And that peace may be in our days in Israel for the days of [the] age:
50.24: To entrust His mercy with us; And let Him deliver us in His time!
50.25: With two nations is my soul vexed, || And the third is no nation:
50.26: They that sit on the mountain of Samaria, and the Philistines, || And that foolish people that dwells in Shechem.
50.27: I have written in this scroll the instruction of understanding and knowledge, || I, Jesus, the son of Sirach Eleazar, of Jerusalem, || Who out of his heart poured out wisdom.
50.28: Blessed is he that will be exercised in these things; And he that lays them up in his heart will become wise.
50.29: For if he does them, he will be strong for all things; For the light of the Lord is his guide.

51.1: I will give thanks to You, O Lord, O King, || And will praise You, O God my Savior: I give thanks to Your Name,
51.2: For You were my protector and helper, || And You delivered my body out of destruction, || And out of the snare of a slanderous tongue, || From lips that forge lies, || And were my helper before those who stood by;
51.3: And You delivered me, || According to the abundance of Your mercy, and [greatness] of Your Name, || From the gnashing [of teeth] ready to devour, || Out of the hand of such as sought my life, || Out of the manifold afflictions which I had;
51.4: From the choking of a fire on every side, || And out of the midst of fire which I did not kindle;
51.5: Out of the depth of the belly of the grave, || And from an unclean tongue, and from lying words—
51.6: The slander of an unrighteous tongue to the king. My soul drew near even to death, || And my life was near to the grave beneath.
51.7: They compassed me on every side, || And there was none to help [me]. I was looking for the help of men, and it was not.
51.8: And I remembered Your mercy, O Lord, || And Your working which has been from perpetuity, || How You deliver those who wait for You, || And save them out of the hand of the enemies.
51.9: And I lifted up my supplication from the earth || And prayed for deliverance from death.
51.10: I called on the Lord, the Father of my Lord, || That He would not forsake me in the days of affliction, || In the time when there was no help against the proud.
51.11: I will praise Your Name continually || And will sing praise with thanksgiving; And my supplication was heard:
51.12: For You saved me from destruction, || And delivered me from the evil time. Therefore, I will give thanks and praise to You, || And bless the Name of the Lord.
51.13: When I was yet young, before I went abroad, || I sought wisdom openly in my prayer.
51.14: Before the temple I asked for her, || And I will seek her out even to the end.
51.15: From [her] flower, as from the ripening grape, || My heart delighted in her; My foot trod in uprightness, || From my youth I tracked her out.
51.16: I bowed my ear down a little, and received her, || And found for myself much instruction.
51.17: I profited in her: To Him that gives me wisdom I will give glory.
51.18: For I purposed to practice her, || And I was zealous for that which is good; And I will never be put to shame.
51.19: My soul has wrestled in her, || And in my doing I was exact: I spread out my hands to the Heaven above || And mourned my ignorance of her.
51.20: I set my soul properly to her, || And in pureness I found her. I got myself a heart [joined] with her from the beginning: Therefore, I will not be forsaken.
51.21: My inward part was also troubled to seek her: Therefore, I have gotten a good possession.
51.22: The Lord gave me a tongue for my reward; And I will praise Him with that.
51.23: Draw near to me, you unlearned, || And lodge in the house of instruction.
51.24: Say, “Why are you lacking in these things, || And your souls are very thirsty?”
51.25: I opened my mouth, and spoke, || “Get her for yourselves without money.
51.26: Put your neck under the yoke, || And let your soul receive instruction: She is hard at hand to find.
51.27: Behold with your eyes, how that I labored but a little, || And found for myself much rest.
51.28: Get instruction with a great sum of silver || And gain much gold by her.
51.29: May your soul rejoice in His mercy, || And may you not be put to shame in praising Him.
51.30: Work your work before the time comes, || And in His time, He will give you your reward.”

1.1: These are the words of the scroll, which Baruch the son of Neriah, the son of Mahseiah, the son of Zedekiah, the son of Hasadiah, the son of Hilkiah, wrote in Babylon,
1.2: in the fifth year, [and] in the seventh day of the month, at the time the Chaldeans took Jerusalem, and burned it with fire.
1.3: And Baruch read the words of this scroll in the hearing of Jehoiachin the son of Jehoiakim king of Judah, and in the hearing of all the people that came to [hear] the scroll,
1.4: and in the hearing of the mighty men, and of the kings’ sons, and in the hearing of the elders, and in the hearing of all the people, from the least to the greatest, even of all those who lived at Babylon by the River Sud.
1.5: And they wept, and fasted, and prayed before the Lord;
1.6: they also made a collection of money according to every man’s power:
1.7: and they sent [it] to Jerusalem to Jehoiakim the [high] priest, the son of Hilkiah, the son of Salom, and to the priests, and to all the people which were found with him at Jerusalem,
1.8: at the same time when he took the vessels of the house of the Lord, that had been carried out of the temple, to return [them] into the land of Judah, the tenth day of the [month] Sivan, [namely,] silver vessels, which Zedekiah the son of Josiah king of Judah had made,
1.9: after Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon had carried away Jehoiachin, and the princes, and the captives, and the mighty men, and the people of the land, from Jerusalem, and brought them to Babylon.
1.10: And they said: “Behold, we have sent you money; therefore buy with the money burnt-offerings, and sin offerings, and incense, and prepare an oblation, and offer on the altar of the Lord our God;
1.11: and pray for the life of Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon, and for the life of his son Belshazzar, that their days may be as the days of Heaven above the earth:
1.12: and the Lord will give us strength, and lighten our eyes, and we will live under the shadow of Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon, and under the shadow of his son Belshazzar, and we will serve them many days, and find favor in their sight.
1.13: Pray for us also to the Lord our God, for we have sinned against the Lord our God; and to this day the wrath of the Lord and His indignation is not turned from us.
1.14: And you will read this scroll which we have sent to you, to make confession in the house of the Lord, on the day of the feast and on the days of the solemn assembly.”
1.15: And you will say: To the Lord our God [belongs] righteousness, but to us confusion of face, as at this day, to the men of Judah, and to the inhabitants of Jerusalem,
1.16: and to our kings, and to our princes, and to our priests, and to our prophets, and to our fathers:
1.17: for that we have sinned before the Lord,
1.18: and disobeyed Him, and have not listened to the voice of the Lord our God, to walk in the commands of the Lord that He has set before us:
1.19: since the day that the Lord brought our fathers out of the land of Egypt, to this present day, we have been disobedient to the Lord our God, and we have dealt unadvisedly in not listening to His voice.
1.20: Why the plagues cleaved to us, and the curse, which the Lord commanded Moses His servant [to pronounce] in the day that He brought our fathers out of the land of Egypt, to give us a land that flows with milk and honey, as at this day.
1.21: Nevertheless, we did not listen to the voice of the Lord our God, according to all the words of the prophets, whom He sent to us,
1.22: but we walked—every man—in the imagination of his own wicked heart, to serve strange gods, and to do that which is evil in the sight of the Lord our God.

2.1: Therefore the Lord has made good His word, which He pronounced against us, and against our judges that judged Israel, and against our kings, and against our princes, and against the men of Israel and Judah,
2.2: to bring on us great plagues, such as never happened under the whole Heaven, as it came to pass in Jerusalem, according to the things that are written in the Law of Moses;
2.3: That we should eat—every man—the flesh of his own son, and every man the flesh of his own daughter.
2.4: Moreover, He has given them to be in subjection to all the kingdoms that are around us, to be a reproach and a desolation among all the surrounding people, where the Lord has scattered them.
2.5: So were they cast down, and not exalted, because we sinned against the Lord our God, in not listening to His voice.
2.6: To the Lord our God [belongs] righteousness, but to us and to our fathers confusion of face, as at this day.
2.7: [For] all these plagues have come on us, which the Lord has pronounced against us.
2.8: Yet we have not entreated the favor of the Lord, in turning everyone from the thoughts of his wicked heart.
2.9: Therefore the Lord has kept watch over the plagues, and the Lord has brought [them] on us; for the Lord is righteous in all His works which He has commanded us.
2.10: Yet we have not listened to His voice, to walk in the commands of the Lord that He has set before us.
2.11: And now, O Lord, You God of Israel, that have brought Your people out of the land of Egypt with a mighty hand, and with signs, and with wonders, and with great power, and with a high arm, and have gotten Yourself a Name, as at this day:
2.12: O Lord our God, we have sinned, we have been ungodly, we have dealt unrighteously in all Your ordinances.
2.13: Let Your wrath turn from us, for we are but a few left among the heathen, where You have scattered us.
2.14: Hear our prayer, O Lord, and our petition, and deliver us for Your own sake, and give us favor in the sight of them which have led us away captive,
2.15: that all the earth may know that You are the Lord our God, because Israel and his posterity is called by Your Name.
2.16: O Lord, look down from Your holy house, and consider us: incline Your ear, O Lord, and hear:
2.17: open Your eyes, and behold, for the dead that are in the grave, whose breath is taken from their bodies, will give to the Lord neither glory nor righteousness:
2.18: but the soul that is greatly vexed, which goes stooping and feeble, and the eyes that fail, and the hungry soul, will give You glory and righteousness, O Lord.
2.19: For we do not present our supplication before You, O Lord our God, for the righteousness of our fathers, and of our kings.
2.20: For You have sent Your wrath and Your indignation on us, as You have spoken by Your servants the prophets, [saying,]
2.21: “The Lord says, Bow your shoulders to serve the king of Babylon, and remain in the land that I gave to your fathers.
2.22: But if you will not hear the voice of the Lord, to serve the king of Babylon,
2.23: I will cause to cease out of the cities of Judah, and from the region near Jerusalem, the voice of mirth, and the voice of gladness, the voice of the bridegroom, and the voice of the bride: and the whole land will be desolate without inhabitant.”
2.24: But we would not listen to Your voice, to serve the king of Babylon: therefore You have made good Your words that You spoke by Your servants the prophets, [namely,] that the bones of our kings, and the bones of our fathers, should be taken out of their places.
2.25: And behold, they are cast out to the heat by day, and to the frost by night, and they died in great miseries by famine, by sword, and by pestilence.
2.26: And the house which is called by Your Name have You laid [waste], as at this day, for the wickedness of the house of Israel and the house of Judah.
2.27: Yet, O Lord our God, You have dealt with us after all Your kindness, and according to all Your great mercy,
2.28: as You spoke by Your servant Moses in the day when You commanded him to write Your law before the sons of Israel, saying,
2.29: “If you will not hear My voice, surely this very great multitude will be turned into a small [number] among the nations, where I will scatter them.
2.30: For I know that they will not hear Me, because it is a stiff-necked people, but in the land of their captivity they will take it to heart,
2.31: and will know that I am the Lord their God: and I will give them a heart, and ears to hear:
2.32: and they will praise Me in the land of their captivity, and think on My Name,
2.33: and will return from their stiff neck, and from their wicked deeds, for they will remember the way of their fathers, which sinned before the Lord.
2.34: And I will bring them again into the land which I swore to their fathers, to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob, and they will be lords of it: and I will increase them, and they will not be diminished.
2.35: And I will make a perpetual covenant with them to be their God, and they will be My people: and I will no longer remove My people Israel out of the land that I have given them.”

3.1: O Lord Almighty, You God of Israel, the soul in anguish, the troubled spirit, cries to You.
3.2: Hear, O Lord, and have mercy; for You are a merciful God: yes, have mercy on us, because we have sinned before You.
3.3: For You sit [as king] forever, and we perish forevermore.
3.4: O Lord Almighty, You God of Israel, hear now the prayer of the dead Israelites, and of the children of them which were sinners before You, that did not listen to the voice of You their God, for this reason these plagues cleaved to us.
3.5: Do not remember the iniquities of our fathers, but remember Your power and Your Name [now] at this time.
3.6: For You are the Lord our God, and You, O Lord, will we praise.
3.7: For this reason You have put Your fear in our hearts, to the intent that we should call on Your Name, and we will praise You in our captivity, for we have called to mind all the iniquity of our fathers, that sinned before You.
3.8: Behold, we are yet this day in our captivity, where You have scattered us, for a reproach and a curse, and to be subject to penalty, according to all the iniquities of our fathers, which departed from the Lord our God.
3.9: Hear, O Israel, the commands of life: give ear to understand wisdom.
3.10: How is it, O Israel, that you are in your enemies’ land, that you have become old in a strange country, that you are defiled with the dead,
3.11: that you are counted with those who [go down] into the grave?
3.12: You have forsaken the fountain of wisdom.
3.13: [For] if you had walked in the way of God, you should have dwelled in peace forever.
3.14: Learn where is wisdom, where is strength, where is understanding; that you may also know where is length of days, and life, where is the light of the eyes, and peace.
3.15: Who has found out her place? A nd who has come into her treasuries?
3.16: Where are the princes of the heathen, and such as ruled the beasts that are on the earth;
3.17: those who had their pastime with the birds of the air, and those who hoarded up silver and gold, wherein men trust; and of whose getting there is no end?
3.18: For those who made in silver, and were so careful, and whose works are past finding out,
3.19: they are vanished and gone down to the grave, and others have come up in their steads.
3.20: Younger men have seen the light and lived on the earth, but the way of knowledge they have not known,
3.21: neither did they understand the paths thereof, neither have their children embraced it: they are far off from their way.
3.22: It has not been heard of in Canaan, neither has it been seen in Teman.
3.23: The sons also of Agar that seek understanding, which are in the land, the merchants of Merran and Teman, and the authors of fables, and the searchers out of understanding; none of these have known the way of wisdom or remembered her paths.
3.24: O Israel, how great is the house of God! A nd how large is the place of His possession!
3.25: [It is] great, and has no end; high, and unmeasurable.
3.26: There were the giants born that were famous of old, great of stature, [and] expert in war.
3.27: God did not choose these, nor did He give the way of knowledge to them;
3.28: so they perished, because they had no wisdom, they perished through their own foolishness.
3.29: Who has gone up into Heaven, and taken her, and brought her down from the clouds?
3.30: Who has gone over the sea, and found her, and will bring her for choice gold?
3.31: There is none that knows her way, nor any that comprehends her path.
3.32: But He that knows all things knows her, He found her out with His understanding: He that prepared the earth forevermore has filled it with four-footed beasts:
3.33: He that sends out the light, and it goes; He called it, and it obeyed Him with fear:
3.34: and the stars shone in their watches, and were glad when He called them, they said, “Here we are.” They shone with gladness to Him that made them.
3.35: This is our God, [and] there will none other be accounted of in comparison of Him.
3.36: He has found out all the way of knowledge, and has given it to His servant Jacob, and to Israel that is beloved of Him.
3.37: Afterward she appeared on earth and was conversant with men.

4.1: This is the scroll of the commands of God, and the law that endures forever: All those who hold it fast [are appointed] to life; but such as leave it will die.
4.2: Turn, O Jacob, and take hold of it: walk toward her shining in the presence of the light thereof.
4.3: Do not give your glory to another, nor the things that are profitable to you to a strange nation.
4.4: O Israel, happy are we, for the things that are pleasing to God are made known to us.
4.5: Be of good cheer, my people, the memorial of Israel.
4.6: You were sold to the nations, [but] not for destruction: because you moved God to wrath, you were delivered to your adversaries.
4.7: For you provoked Him that made you by sacrificing to demons, and not to God.
4.8: You forgot the perpetual God that brought you up; you also grieved Jerusalem that nursed you.
4.9: For she saw the wrath that has come on you from God, and said, “Listen, you [women] that dwell about Zion, for God has brought on me great mourning;
4.10: for I have seen the captivity of my sons and daughters, which the Perpetual has brought on them.
4.11: For with joy Inourished them, but sent them away with weeping and mourning.
4.12: Let no man rejoice over me, a widow, and forsaken of many, for the sins of my children I am left desolate, because they turned away from the law of God,
4.13: and had no regard to His statutes, neither did they walk in the ways of God’s commands, nor trod in the paths of discipline in His righteousness.
4.14: Let those who dwell about Zion come, and remember the captivity of my sons and daughters, which the Perpetual has brought on them.
4.15: For He has brought a nation on them from far, a shameless nation, and of a strange language, who neither gave reverence to old man, nor pitied child.
4.16: And they have carried away the dear beloved sons of the widow and left her that was alone desolate of her daughters.
4.17: But I—how can I help you?
4.18: For He that brought these plagues on you will deliver you from the hand of your enemies.
4.19: Go your way, O my children, go your way, for I am left desolate.
4.20: I have put off the garment of peace and put on myself the sackcloth of my petition: I will cry to the Perpetual as long as I live.
4.21: Be of good cheer, O my children, cry to God, and He will deliver you from the power and hand of the enemies.
4.22: For I have trusted in the Perpetual, that He will save you; and joy has come to me from the Holy One, because of the mercy which will soon come to you from your continuous Savior.
4.23: For I sent you out with mourning and weeping, but God will give you to me again with joy and gladness forever.
4.24: For like as now those who dwell about Zion have seen your captivity, so they will shortly see your salvation from our God, which will come on you with great glory, and brightness of the Perpetual.
4.25: My children, suffer patiently the wrath that has come on you from God, for your enemy has persecuted you; but shortly you will see his destruction, and will tread on their necks.
4.26: My delicate ones have gone rough ways; they were taken away as a flock carried off by the enemies.
4.27: Be of good cheer, O my children, and cry to God, for you will be remembered of Him that has brought [these things] on you.
4.28: For as it was your mind to go astray from God, return and seek Him ten times more.
4.29: For He that brought these plagues on you will bring you continuous joy again with your salvation.”
4.30: Be of good cheer, O Jerusalem, for He that called you by name will comfort you.
4.31: Miserable are those who afflicted you and rejoiced at your fall.
4.32: Miserable are the cities which your children served; miserable is she that received your sons.
4.33: For as she rejoiced at your fall, and was glad of your ruin, so she will be grieved for her own desolation.
4.34: And I will take away her exultation in her great multitude, and her boasting will be turned into mourning.
4.35: For fire will come on her from the Perpetual, long to endure; and she will be inhabited of devils for a great time.
4.36: O Jerusalem look around you toward the east and behold the joy that comes to you from God.
4.37: Behold, your sons come, whom you sent away, they come gathered together from the east to the west at the word of the Holy One, rejoicing in the glory of God.

5.1: Put off, O Jerusalem, the garment of your mourning and affliction, and put on the beauty of the glory that [comes] from God forever.
5.2: Cast about you the robe of the righteousness which [comes] from God; set a diadem on your head of the glory of the Perpetual.
5.3: For God will show your brightness to every [region] under Heaven.
5.4: For your name will be called of God forever, The Peace of Righteousness, and, The Glory of Godliness.
5.5: Arise, O Jerusalem, and stand on the height, and look around you toward the east, and behold your children gathered from the going down of the sun to the rising thereof at the word of the Holy One, rejoicing that God has remembered them.
5.6: For they went from you on foot, being led away of their enemies, but God brings them in to you, borne on high with glory, as [on] a royal throne.
5.7: For God has appointed that every high mountain, and the perpetual hills, should be made low, and the valleys filled up, to make plain the ground, that Israel may go safely in the glory of God.
5.8: Moreover the woods and every sweet-smelling tree have overshadowed Israel by the command of God.
5.9: For God will lead Israel with joy in the light of His glory with the mercy and righteousness that comes from Him.

6.1: A copy of a letter, which Jeremiah sent to them which were to be led captives into Babylon by the king of the Babylonians, to certify them, as it was commanded him of God.
6.2: Because of the sins which you have committed before God, you will be led away captives to Babylon by Nebuchadnezzar king of the Babylonians.
6.3: So when you come to Babylon, you will remain there many years, and for a long season, even for seven generations: and after that I will bring you out peaceably from there.
6.4: But now you will see in Babylon gods of silver, and of gold, and of wood, borne on shoulders, which cause the nations to fear.
6.5: Beware, therefore, that you in no way become like to the strangers, neither let fear take hold on you because of them, when you see the multitude before them and behind them, worshiping them.
6.6: But say in your hearts, “O Lord, we must worship You.”
6.7: For My messenger is with you, and I Myself care for your souls.
6.8: For their tongue is polished by the workman, and they themselves are overlaid with gold and with silver; yet they are but false and cannot speak.
6.9: And taking gold, as it were for a virgin that loves to be happy, they make crowns for the heads of their gods:
6.10: and sometimes also the priests carry from their gods gold and silver and bestow it on themselves;
6.11: and will even give thereof to the common prostitutes: and they deck them as men with garments, [even] the gods of silver, and gods of gold, and of wood.
6.12: Yet these gods cannot save themselves from rust and moths, though they are covered with purple raiment.
6.13: They wipe their faces because of the dust of the temple, which is thick on them.
6.14: And he that cannot put to death one that offends against him holds a scepter, as though he were judge of a country.
6.15: He also has a dagger in his right hand, and an axe, but cannot deliver himself from war and robbers.
6.16: Whereby they are not known to be gods: therefore, do not fear them.
6.17: For like as a vessel that a man uses is worth nothing when it is broken, even so it is with their gods: when they are set up in the temples their eyes are full of dust through the feet of those who come in.
6.18: And as the courts are made sure on every side on him that offends the king, as being committed to suffer death, [even so] the priests make fast their temples with doors, with locks, and bars, lest they are carried off by robbers.
6.19: They light the candles, yes, more than for themselves, of which they cannot see one.
6.20: They are as one of the beams of the temple; and men say their hearts are eaten out, when things creeping out of the earth devour both them and their raiment:
6.21: they do not feel it when their faces are blackened through the smoke that comes out of the temple:
6.22: bats, swallows, and birds land on their bodies and heads; and in like manner the cats also.
6.23: Whereby you may know that they are no gods: therefore, do not fear them.
6.24: Notwithstanding the gold with which they are beset to make them beautiful, except one wipe off the rust, they will not shine, for not even when they were molten did they feel it.
6.25: Things wherein there is no breath are bought at any cost.
6.26: Having no feet, they are borne on shoulders, whereby they declare to men that they are worth nothing.
6.27: They also that serve them are ashamed, for if they fall to the ground at any time, they cannot rise up again of themselves; neither, if they are bowed down, can they make themselves straight, but the offerings are set before them, as if they were dead men.
6.28: And the things that are sacrificed to them, their priests sell and spend; and in like manner their wives also lay up part thereof in salt; but to the poor and to the impotent they will give nothing thereof.
6.29: The menstruous woman and the woman in childbirth touch their sacrifices, knowing, therefore, by these things, that they are not gods, [and so] do not fear them.
6.30: For how can they be called gods? B ecause women set meat before the gods of silver, gold, and wood.
6.31: And in their temples the priests sit on seats, having their clothes torn, and their heads and beards shaven, and nothing on their heads.
6.32: They roar and cry before their gods, as men do at the feast when one is dead.
6.33: The priests also take off garments from them and clothe their wives and children in addition.
6.34: Whether it be evil that one does to them, or good, they are not able to repay it: they can neither set up a king, nor put him down.
6.35: In like manner, they can neither give riches nor money; though a man makes a vow to them, and does not keep it, they will never exact it.
6.36: They can save no man from death, neither deliver the weak from the mighty.
6.37: They cannot restore a blind man to his sight, nor deliver any that is in distress.
6.38: They can show no mercy to the widow, nor do good to the fatherless.
6.39: They are like the stones that are [hewn] out of the mountain, [these gods] of wood, and that are overlaid with gold and with silver. Those who minister to them will be confounded.
6.40: How should a man then think or say that they are gods, when even the Chaldeans themselves dishonor them?
6.41: Who if they will see one mute that cannot speak, they bring him, and entreat him to call on Bel, as though he were able to understand.
6.42: Yet they cannot perceive this themselves and forsake them, for they have no understanding.
6.43: The women also with cords around them sit in the ways, burning bran for incense, but if any of them, drawn by some that passes by, lies with him, she reproaches her fellow, that she was not thought as worthy as herself, nor her cord broken.
6.44: Whatever is done among them is false. How should a man then think or say that they are gods?
6.45: They are fashioned by carpenters and goldsmiths—they can be nothing else than the workmen will have them to be.
6.46: And they themselves that fashioned them can never continue long; how then should the things that are fashioned by them?
6.47: For they have left lies and reproaches to those who come after.
6.48: For when there comes any war or plague on them, the priests consult with themselves, where they may be hidden with them.
6.49: How then can men not understand that they are not gods, which can neither save themselves from war, nor from plague?
6.50: For seeing they are but of wood, and overlaid with gold and with silver, it will be known hereafter that they are false;
6.51: and it will be manifest to all nations and kings that they are no gods, but the works of men’s hands, and that there is no work of God in them.
6.52: Who then may not know that they are no gods?
6.53: For neither can they set up a king in a land, nor give rain to men.
6.54: Neither can they judge their own cause, nor redress a wrong, being unable, for they are as crows between the heavens and earth.
6.55: For even when fire falls on the house of gods of wood, or overlaid with gold or with silver, their priests will flee away, and escape, but they themselves will be burned apart like beams.
6.56: Moreover they cannot withstand any king or enemies. How should a man then allow or think that they are gods?
6.57: Neither are those gods of wood, and overlaid with silver or with gold, able to escape either from thieves or robbers.
6.58: Whose gold, and silver, and garments with which they are clothed, they that are strong will take from them, and go away with [them]; neither will they be able to help themselves.
6.59: Therefore it is better to be a king that shows his manhood, or else a vessel in a house profitable for that of which the owner will have need, than such false gods; or even a door in a house, to keep the things safe that are therein, than such false gods; or a pillar of wood in a palace, than such false gods.
6.60: For sun, and moon, and stars, being bright and sent to do their offices, are obedient.
6.61: Likewise also, the lightning when it glitters is fair to see; and after the same manner the wind also blows in every country.
6.62: And when God commands the clouds to go over the whole world, they do as they are told.
6.63: And the fire sent from above to consume mountains and woods does as it is commanded, but these are to be likened to them neither in show nor power.
6.64: Why a man should neither think nor say that they are gods, seeing they are neither able to judge causes, nor to do good to men.
6.65: Knowing, therefore, that they are not gods, do not fear them.
6.66: For they can neither curse nor bless kings;
6.67: neither can they show signs in the heavens among the nations, nor shine as the sun, nor give light as the moon.
6.68: The beasts are better than they, for they can get under a covert and help themselves.
6.69: In no way then is it manifest to us that they are gods. Therefore, do not fear them.
6.70: For as a scarecrow in a garden of cucumbers that keeps nothing, so are their gods of wood, and overlaid with gold and with silver.
6.71: Likewise also, their gods of wood, and overlaid with gold and with silver, are like to a white thorn in an orchard, that every bird sits on; as also to a dead body that is cast out into the dark.
6.72: And you will know them to be no gods by the bright purple that rots on them: And they themselves afterward will be consumed and will be a reproach in the country.
6.73: Better therefore is the just man that has no idols, for he will be far from reproach.

1.1: It came to pass, after that Alexander the Macedonian, the son of Philip, who came out of the land of Chittim, and struck Darius king of the Persians and Medes, [it came to pass,] after he had struck him, that he reigned in his stead, in former time, over Greece.
1.2: And he fought many battles, and won many strongholds, and killed the kings of the earth,
1.3: and went through to the ends of the earth and took spoils of a multitude of nations. And the earth was quiet before him, and he was exalted, and his heart was lifted up,
1.4: and he gathered together an exceedingly strong army, and ruled over countries and nations and principalities, and they became tributary to him.
1.5: And after these things he fell sick, and perceived that he should die.
1.6: And he called his servants, which were honorable, which had been brought up with him from his youth, and he divided to them his kingdom, while he was yet alive.
1.7: And Alexander reigned twelve years, and he died.
1.8: And his servants bare rule, each one in his place.
1.9: And they did all put diadems on themselves after that he was dead, and so did their sons after them many years: and they multiplied evils in the earth.
1.10: And there came out of them a sinful root, Antiochus Epiphanes, son of Antiochus the king, who had been a hostage at Rome, and he reigned in the one hundred and thirty- seventh year of the kingdom of the Greeks.
1.11: In those days came there out of Israel transgressors of the law, and persuaded many, saying, let us go and make a covenant with the nations that are around us; for since we were parted from them many evils have befallen us.
1.12: And the saying was good in their eyes.
1.13: And certain of the people were forward [here in] land went to the king, and he gave them license to do after the ordinances of the nations.
1.14: And they built a place of exercise in Jerusalem according to the laws of the nations;
1.15: and they made themselves uncircumcised, and forsook the holy covenant, and joined themselves to the nations, and sold themselves to do evil.
1.16: And the kingdom was well ordered in the sight of Antiochus, and he thought to reign over Egypt, that he might reign over the two kingdoms.
1.17: And he entered into Egypt with a great multitude, with chariots, and with elephants, and with horsemen, and with a great navy;
1.18: and he made war against Ptolemy king of Egypt; and Ptolemy was put to shame before him and fled; and many fell wounded to death.
1.19: And they got possession of the strong cities in the land of Egypt; and he took the spoils of Egypt.
1.20: And Antiochus, after he had struck Egypt, returned in the one hundred and forty-third year, and went up against Israel and Jerusalem with a great multitude,
1.21: and entered presumptuously into the sanctuary, and took the golden altar, and the candlestick of the light, and all that pertained to that,
1.22: and the table of the Bread of the Presentation, and the cups to pour with, and the bowls, and the golden censers, and the veil, and the crowns, and the adorning of gold which was on the face of the temple, and he peeled it all off.
1.23: And he took the silver and the gold and the precious vessels; and he took the hidden treasures which he found.
1.24: And when he had taken all, he went away into his own land, and he made a great slaughter, and spoke very presumptuously.
1.25: And there came great mourning on Israel, in every place where they were;
1.26: and the rulers and elders groaned, the virgins and young men were made feeble, and the beauty of the women was changed.
1.27: Every bridegroom took up lamentation, she that sat in the marriage chamber was in heaviness.
1.28: And the land was moved for the inhabitants thereof, and all the house of Jacob was clothed with shame.
1.29: And after two full years the king sent a chief collector of tribute to the cities of Judah, and he came to Jerusalem with a great multitude.
1.30: And he spoke words of peace to them in subtlety, and they gave him credence: and he fell on the city suddenly, and struck it very severely, and destroyed much people out of Israel.
1.31: And he took the spoils of the city, and set it on fire, and pulled down the houses thereof and the walls thereof on every side.
1.32: And they led captive the women and the children, and the cattle they took in possession.
1.33: And they built the city of David with a great and strong wall, with strong towers, and it became to them a citadel.
1.34: And they put there a sinful nation, transgressors of the law, and they strengthened themselves therein.
1.35: And they stored up arms and food, and gathering together the spoils of Jerusalem, they laid them up there, and they became a severe snare:
1.36: and it became a place to lie in wait in against the sanctuary, and an evil adversary to Israel continually.
1.37: And they shed innocent blood on every side of the sanctuary and defiled the sanctuary.
1.38: And the inhabitants of Jerusalem fled because of them; and she became a habitation of strangers, and she became strange to those who were born in her, and her children forsook her.
1.39: Her sanctuary was laid waste like a wilderness, her feasts were turned into mourning, her Sabbaths into reproach, her honor into contempt.
1.40: According to her glory, so was her dishonor multiplied, and her high estate was turned into mourning.
1.41: And King Antiochus wrote to his whole kingdom, that all should be one people,
1.42: and that each should forsake his own laws. And all the nations agreed according to the word of the king;
1.43: and many of Israel consented to his worship, and sacrificed to the idols, and profaned the Sabbath.
1.44: And the king sent letters by the hand of messengers to Jerusalem and the cities of Judah, that they should follow laws strange to the land,
1.45: and should forbid whole burnt-offerings and sacrifice and drink offerings in the sanctuary; and should profane the Sabbaths and feasts,
1.46: and pollute the sanctuary and those who were holy;
1.47: that they should build altars, and temples, and shrines for idols, and should sacrifice swine’s flesh and unclean beasts:
1.48: and that they should leave their sons uncircumcised, that they should make their souls abominable with all manner of uncleanness and profanation;
1.49: so that they might forget the law, and change all the ordinances.
1.50: And whoever will not do according to the word of the king, he will die.
1.51: According to all these words wrote he to his whole kingdom; and he appointed overseers over all the people, and he commanded the cities of Judah to sacrifice, city by city.
1.52: And from the people were gathered together to them many, everyone that had forsaken the law; and they did evil things in the land;
1.53: and they made Israel to hide themselves in every place of refuge which they had.
1.54: And on the fifteenth day of Kislev, in the one hundred and forty-fifth year, they built an abomination of desolation on the altar, and in the cities of Judah on every side they built [idol] altars.
1.55: And at the doors of the houses and in the streets they burned incense.
1.56: And they tore in pieces the scrolls of the law which they found and set them on fire.
1.57: And wherever was found with any a scroll of the Covenant, and if any consented to the law, the king’s sentence delivered him to death.
1.58: So did they in their might to Israel, to those that were found month by month in the cities.
1.59: And on the twenty-fifth day of the month they sacrificed on the [idol] altar [of God].
1.60: And the women that had circumcised their children they put to death according to the command.
1.61: And they hanged their babies about their necks, and [destroyed] their houses, and those who had circumcised them.
1.62: And many in Israel were fully resolved and confirmed in themselves not to eat unclean things.
1.63: And they chose to die, that they might not be defiled with the meats, and that they might not profane the holy covenant: and they died.
1.64: And there came exceedingly great wrath on Israel.

2.1: In those days Mattathias rose up, the son of John, the son of Simeon, a priest of the sons of Joarib, from Jerusalem; and he lived at Modin.
2.2: And he had five sons, John, who was surnamed Gaddis;
2.3: Simon, who was called Thassi;
2.4: Judas, who was called Maccabaeus;
2.5: Eleazar, who was called Avaran; Jonathan, who was called Apphus.
2.6: And he saw the blasphemies that were committed in Judah and in Jerusalem,
2.7: and he said, “Woe is me! W hy was I born to see the destruction of my people, and the destruction of the holy city, and to dwell there, when it was given into the hand of the enemy, the sanctuary into the hand of aliens?
2.8: Her temple is become as a man that was glorious:
2.9: her vessels of glory are carried away into captivity, her infants are slain in her streets, her young men with the sword of the enemy.
2.10: What nation has not inherited her palaces, and gotten possession of her spoils?
2.11: Her adorning is all taken away; instead of a free woman she is become a bond woman:
2.12: and behold, our holy things and our beauty and our glory are laid waste, and the nations have profaned them.
2.13: Why should we live any longer?”
2.14: And Mattathias and his sons tore their clothes, and put on sackcloth, and mourned exceedingly.
2.15: And the king’s officers, that were enforcing the apostasy, came into the city Modin to sacrifice.
2.16: And many of Israel came to them, and Mattathias and his sons were gathered together.
2.17: And the king’s officers answered and spoke to Mattathias, saying, “You are a ruler and an honorable and great man in this city, and strengthened with sons and relatives:
2.18: now therefore, come first and do the command of the king, as all the nations have done, and the men of Judah, and those who remain in Jerusalem: and you and your house will be in the number of the king’s friends, and you and your sons will be honored with silver and gold and many gifts.”
2.19: And Mattathias answered and said with a loud voice, “If all the nations that are in the house of the king’s dominion listen to him, to each one fall away from the worship of his fathers, and have made choice to follow his commands,
2.20: yet I and my sons and my relatives will walk in the covenant of our fathers.
2.21: Heaven forbid that we should forsake the law and the ordinances.
2.22: We will not listen to the king’s words, to go aside from our worship, on the right hand, or on the left.”
2.23: And when he had left speaking these words, there came a Jew in the sight of all to sacrifice on the altar which was at Modin, according to the king’s command.
2.24: And Mattathias saw it, and his zeal was kindled, and his reins trembled, and he showed out his wrath according to judgment, and ran, and killed him on the altar.
2.25: And the king’s officer, who compelled men to sacrifice, he killed at that time, and pulled down the altar.
2.26: And he was zealous for the law, even as Phinehas did to Zimri the son of Salu.
2.27: And Mattathias cried out in the city with a loud voice, saying, “Whoever is zealous for the law, and maintains the covenant, let him come out after me.”
2.28: And he and his sons fled into the mountains and forsook all that they had in the city.
2.29: Then many that sought after justice and judgment went down into the wilderness, to dwell there,
2.30: they, and their sons, and their wives, and their cattle; because evils were multiplied on them.
2.31: And it was told the king’s officers, and the forces that were in Jerusalem, the city of David, that certain men, who had broken the king’s command, were gone down into the secret places in the wilderness;
2.32: and many pursued after them, and having overtaken them, they encamped against them, and set the battle in array against them on the Sabbath day.
2.33: And they said to them, so far, “Come out, and do according to the word of the king, and you will live.”
2.34: And they said, “We will not come out, neither will we do the word of the king, to profane the Sabbath day.”
2.35: And they hurried to give them battle.
2.36: And they did not answer them, neither did they cast a stone at them, nor stopped up the secret places,
2.37: saying, “Let us die all in our innocence: the heavens and earth witness over us, that you put us to death without trial.”
2.38: And they rose up against them in battle on the Sabbath, and they died, they and their wives and their children, and their cattle, to the number of one thousand souls.
2.39: And Mattathias and his friends knew it, and they mourned over them exceedingly.
2.40: And one said to another, “If we all do as our countrymen have done, and fight not against the nations for our lives and our ordinances, they will now quickly destroy us from off the earth.”
2.41: And they took counsel on that day, saying, “Whoever will come against us to battle on the Sabbath day, let us fight against him, and we will in no way all die, as our countrymen died in the secret places.”
2.42: Then were gathered together to them a company of Hasidaeans, mighty men of Israel, everyone that offered himself willingly for the law.
2.43: And all those who fled from the evils were added to them and became a stay to them.
2.44: And they mustered an army, and struck sinners in their anger, and lawless men in their wrath: and the rest fled to the nations for safety.
2.45: And Mattathias and his friends went around and pulled down the altars;
2.46: and they circumcised by force the children that were uncircumcised, as many as they found in the coasts of Israel.
2.47: And they pursued after the sons of pride, and the work prospered in their hand.
2.48: And they rescued the law out of the hand of the nations, and out of the hand of the kings, neither suffered they the sinner to triumph.
2.49: And the days of Mattathias drew near that he should die, and he said to his sons, “Now have pride and rebuke gotten strength, and a season of overthrow, and wrath of indignation.
2.50: And now, my children, be zealous for the law, and give your lives for the covenant of your fathers.
2.51: And call to remembrance the deeds of our fathers which they did in their generations; and receive great glory and a perpetual name.
2.52: Was not Abraham found faithful in temptation, and it was reckoned to him for righteousness?
2.53: Joseph in the time of his distress kept the command and became lord of Egypt.
2.54: Phinehas our father, for that he was zealous exceedingly, obtained the covenant of a perpetual priesthood.
2.55: Joshua for fulfilling the word became a judge in Israel.
2.56: Caleb for bearing witness in the congregation obtained a heritage in the land.
2.57: David for being merciful inherited the throne of a kingdom forever and ever.
2.58: Elijah, for that he was exceedingly zealous for the law, was taken up into Heaven.
2.59: Hananiah, Azariah, Mishael, believed, and were saved out of the flame.
2.60: Daniel for his innocence was delivered from the mouth of lions.
2.61: And so consider you from generation to generation, that none that put their trust in him will lack for strength.
2.62: And do not be afraid of the words of a sinful man; for his glory will be dung and worms.
2.63: Today he will be lifted up, and tomorrow he will in no way be found, because he is returned to his dust, and his thought is perished.
2.64: And you, my children, be strong, and show yourselves men in behalf of the law; for therein will you obtain glory.
2.65: And behold, Simon your brother, I know that he is a man of counsel; give ear to him always: he will be a father to you.
2.66: And Judas Maccabaeus, he has been strong and mighty from his youth: he will be your captain and will fight the battle of the people.
2.67: And take to yourself all the doers of the law and avenge the wrong of your people.
2.68: Render a repayment to the nations and take heed to the commands of the law.”
2.69: And he blessed them and was gathered to his fathers.
2.70: And he died in the one hundred and forty-sixth year, and his sons buried him in the tombs of his fathers at Modin, and all Israel made great lamentation for him.

3.1: And his son Judas, who was called Maccabaeus, rose up in his stead.
3.2: And all his countrymen helped him, and so did all those who cleaved to his father, and they fought with gladness the battle of Israel.
3.3: And he got his people great glory, and put on a breastplate as a giant, and girded his warlike harness about him, and set battles in array, protecting the army with his sword.
3.4: And he was like a lion in his deeds, and as a lion’s whelp roaring for prey.
3.5: And he pursued the lawless, seeking them out, and he burned up those that troubled his people.
3.6: And the lawless shrunk for fear of him, and all the workers of lawlessness were severely troubled, and salvation prospered in his hand.
3.7: And he angered many kings, and made Jacob glad with his acts, and his memorial is blessed forever.
3.8: And he went around among the cities of Judah, and destroyed the ungodly out of the land, and turned away wrath from Israel:
3.9: and he was renowned to the utmost part of the earth, and he gathered together such as were ready to perish.
3.10: And Apollonius gathered the nations together, and a great army from Samaria, to fight against Israel.
3.11: And Judas perceived it, and he went out to meet him, and struck him, and killed him: and many fell wounded to death, and the rest fled.
3.12: And they took their spoils, and Judas took the sword of Apollonius, and with that he fought all his days.
3.13: And Seron, the commander of the army of Syria, heard it said that Judas had gathered a gathering and a congregation of faithful men with him, and of such as went out to war;
3.14: And he said, I will make myself a name and get me glory in the kingdom; and I will fight against Judas and those who are with him, that set at nothing the word of the king.
