Similitude 9.204: Therefore, if they remain under the dominion of the same desire, they are dead, and there is no hope of life for them; but if they convert, and finish their ministry in a holy manner, they will be able to live.
Similitude 9.205: And they who were covered with scabs are those who have denied their Lord, and have not returned to Him again; but becoming withered and desert-like, and not cleaving to the servants of God, but living in solitude, they destroy their own souls.
Similitude 9.206: For as a vine, when left within an enclosure, and meeting with neglect, is destroyed, and is made desolate by the weeds, and in time grows wild, and is no longer of any use to its master, so also are such men—as have given themselves up and become useless to their Lord—from having contracted savage habits.
Similitude 9.207: Therefore, these men have conversion in their power, unless they are found to have denied from the heart; but if anyone is found to have denied from the heart, I do not know if he may live.
Similitude 9.208: And I say this not for these present days, in order that anyone who has denied may obtain conversion, for It is impossible for him to be saved who now intends to deny his Lord; but to those who denied Him long ago, conversion seems to be possible.
Similitude 9.209: Therefore, if anyone intends to convert, let him do so quickly, before the tower is completed; for if not, he will be utterly destroyed by the women.
Similitude 9.210: For as wild beasts destroy and kill a man by their poison, so also do the words of such men destroy and ruin a man. These, accordingly, are mutilated in their faith, on account of the deeds which they have done in themselves; yet some converted, and were saved.
Similitude 9.211: And the rest, who are of such a character, can be saved if they convert; but if they do not convert, they will perish with those women, whose strength they have assumed.
Similitude 9.212: And from the tenth mountain, where trees were which overshadowed certain sheep, they who believed were the following: overseers given to hospitality, who always gladly received into their houses the servants of God, without dissimulation.
Similitude 9.213: And the overseers never failed to protect, by their service, the widows, and those who were in want, and always maintained a holy conversation.
Similitude 9.214: All these, accordingly, will be protected by the Lord forever. They who do these things are honorable before God, and their place is already with the messengers, if they remain serving God to the end.
Similitude 9.215: And from the eleventh mountain, where trees were full of fruits, adorned with fruits of various kinds, they who believed were the following: they who suffered for the Name of the Son of God, and who also suffered cheerfully with their whole heart, and laid down their lives.”
Similitude 9.216: “Why, then, Lord,” I said, “do all these trees bear fruit, and some of them—fairer than the rest?”
Similitude 9.217: “Listen,” He said: “all who once suffered for the Name of the Lord are honorable before God; and of all these the sins were remitted, because they suffered for the Name of the Son of God. And why their fruits are of various kinds, and some of them superior, listen.
Similitude 9.218: All,” He continued, “who were brought before the authorities and were examined, and did not deny, but suffered cheerfully—these are held in greater honor with God, and of these the fruit is superior;
Similitude 9.219: but all who were cowards, and in doubt, and who reasoned in their hearts whether they would deny or confess, and yet suffered, of these the fruit is less, because that suggestion came into their hearts; for that suggestion—that a servant should deny his Lord—is evil.
Similitude 9.220: Have a care, therefore, you who are planning such things, lest that suggestion remain in your hearts, and you perish to God.
Similitude 9.221: And you who suffer for His Name ought to glorify God, because He deemed you worthy to bear His Name, that all your sins might be healed. [[ Therefore, rather deem yourselves happy ]], and think that you have done a great thing, if any of you suffer on account of God.
Similitude 9.222: The Lord bestows life on you, and you do not understand, for your sins were heavy; but if you had not suffered for the Name of the Lord, you would have died to God on account of your sins.
Similitude 9.223: These things I say to you who are hesitating about denying or confessing: acknowledge that you have the Lord, lest, denying Him, you are delivered up to prison.
Similitude 9.224: If the heathen punishes their slaves when one of them denies his master, what do you think your Lord will do, who has authority over all men? Put away these counsels out of your hearts, that you may continually live to God.
Similitude 9.225: And they who believed from the twelfth mountain, which was white, are the following: they are as infant children, in whose hearts no evil originates; nor did they know what wickedness is, but always remained as children.
Similitude 9.226: Such accordingly, without doubt, dwell in the Kingdom of God, because they defiled the commandments of God in nothing; but they remained like children all the days of their life in the same mind.
Similitude 9.227: All of you, then, who will remain steadfast, and be as children, without doing evil, will be more honored than all who have been previously mentioned; for all infants are honorable before God, and are the first persons with Him.
Similitude 9.228: Blessed, then, are you who put away wickedness from yourselves, and put on innocence. You will live to God as the first of all.”
Similitude 9.229: After He had finished the similitudes of the mountains, I said to Him, “Lord, now explain to me about the stones that were taken out of the plain, and put into the building instead of the stones that were taken out of the tower; and about the round stones that were put into the building; and those that still remain round.”
Similitude 9.230: “Hear,” He answered, “about all these also. The stones taken out of the plain and put into the building of the tower instead of those that were rejected, are the roots of this white mountain.
Similitude 9.231: Therefore, when they who believed from the white mountain were all found guileless, the Lord of the tower commanded those from the roots of this mountain to be cast into the building of the tower;
Similitude 9.232: for He knew that if these stones were to go to the building of the tower, they would remain bright, and not one of them would become black.
Similitude 9.233: But if He had so resolved with respect to the other mountains, it would have been necessary for Him to visit that tower again, and to cleanse it.
Similitude 9.234: Now all these persons were found white who believed, and who will yet believe, for they are of the same race. This is a happy race, because it is innocent. Hear now, further, about these round and shining stones.
Similitude 9.235: All these are also from the white mountain. Hear, moreover, why they were found round: because their riches had obscured and darkened them a little from the truth, although they never departed from God; nor did any evil word proceed out of their mouth, but all justice, virtue, and truth.
Similitude 9.236: When the Lord, therefore, saw the mind of these persons, that they were born good, and could be good, He ordered their riches to be cut down, not to be taken away forever, that they might be able to do some good with what was left them; and they will live to God, because they are of a good race.
Similitude 9.237: Therefore, they were rounded a little by the chisel, and put in the building of the tower.
Similitude 9.238: But the other round stones, which had not yet been adapted to the building of the tower, and had not yet received the seal, were for this reason put back into their place, because they are exceedingly round.
Similitude 9.239: Now this age must be cut down in these things, and in the vanities of their riches, and then they will meet in the Kingdom of God; for they must of necessity enter into the Kingdom of God, because the Lord has blessed this innocent race.
Similitude 9.240: Of this race, therefore, no one will perish; for although any of them might be tempted by the most wicked devil, and commit sin, he will quickly return to his Lord.
Similitude 9.241: I deem you happy, I, who am the Messenger of conversion, whoever of you are innocent as children, because your part is good, and honorable before God.
Similitude 9.242: Moreover, I say to you all, who have received the seal of the Son of God, be clothed with simplicity, and do not be mindful of offenses, nor remain in wickedness.
Similitude 9.243: Therefore, lay aside the recollection of your offenses and bitternesses, and you will be formed in one spirit.
Similitude 9.244: And heal and take away from you those wicked schisms, that if the Lord of the flocks comes, He may rejoice concerning you. And He will rejoice if He finds all things sound, and none of you will perish.
Similitude 9.245: But if He finds any one of these sheep strayed, woe to the shepherds! And if the shepherds themselves have strayed, what answer will they give Him for their flocks?
Similitude 9.246: Will they perhaps say that they were harassed by their flocks? They will not be believed, for the thing is incredible that a shepherd could suffer from his flock; rather, he will be punished on account of his falsehood.
Similitude 9.247: And I myself am a shepherd, and I am under a most stringent necessity of rendering an account of you.
Similitude 9.248: Therefore heal yourselves while the tower is still building. The Lord dwells in men that love peace, because He loved peace; but from the contentious and the utterly wicked He is far distant.
Similitude 9.249: Restore to Him, therefore, a spirit as sound as you received it.
Similitude 9.250: For when you have given a new garment to a fuller, and desire to receive it back whole at the end, if, then, the fuller returns a torn garment to you, will you take it from him, and not rather be angry, and abuse him, saying, I gave you a garment that was whole: why have you rent it, and made it useless, so that it can be of no use on account of the rent which you have made in it?
Similitude 9.251: Would you not say all this to the fuller about the rent which you found in your garment? Therefore, if you grieve about your garment, and complain because you have not received it whole, what do you think the Lord will do to you, who gave you a sound spirit, which you have rendered altogether useless, so that it can be of no service to its possessor?
Similitude 9.252: F or its use began to be unprofitable, seeing it was corrupted by you. Therefore, will the Lord, because of this conduct of yours regarding His Spirit, not act in the same way, and deliver you over to death?
Similitude 9.253: Assuredly, I say, He will do the same to all those whom He will find retaining a recollection of offenses.
Similitude 9.254: Do not trample His mercy under foot, He says, but rather honor Him, because He is so patient with your sins, and is not as you are. Convert, for it is useful to you.
Similitude 9.255: All these things which are written above, I, the Shepherd, the Messenger of conversion, have showed and spoken to the servants of God.
Similitude 9.256: Therefore, if you believe, and listen to My words, and walk in them, and amend your ways, you will have it in your power to live: but if you remain in wickedness, and in the recollection of offenses, no sinner of that class will live to God.
Similitude 9.257: All these words which I had to say have been spoken to you.” The Shepherd said to me, “Have you asked Me everything?” And I replied, “Yes, Lord.”
Similitude 9.258: “Why did you not ask Me about the shape of the stones that were put into the building, that I might explain to you why we filled up the shapes?” And I said, “I forgot, Lord.”
Similitude 9.259: “Hear now, then,” He said, “about this also. These are they who have now heard My commandments and converted with their whole hearts.
Similitude 9.260: And when the Lord saw that their conversion was good and pure, and that they were able to remain in it, He ordered their former sins to be blotted out. For these shapes were their sins, and they were leveled down, that they might not appear.”

Similitude 10.1: After I had fully written down this scroll, that messenger who had delivered me to the Shepherd came into the house in which I was, and sat down on a couch, and the Shepherd stood on his right hand.
Similitude 10.2: He then called me, and spoke to me as follows: “I have delivered you and your house to the Shepherd, that you may be protected by Him.” “Yes, Lord,” I said.
Similitude 10.3: “If you wish, therefore, to be protected,” he said, “from all annoyance, and from all harsh treatment, and to have success in every good work and word, and to possess all the virtues of righteousness, walk in these commandments which He has given you, and you will be able to subdue all wickedness.
Similitude 10.4: For if you keep those commandments, every desire and pleasure of the world will be subject to you, and success will attend to you in every good work.
Similitude 10.5: Take to yourself His experience and moderation, and say to all that He is in great honor and dignity with God, and that He is a president with great power, and mighty in His office.
Similitude 10.6: To Him alone throughout the whole world is the power of conversion assigned. Does He seem to you to be powerful?
Similitude 10.7: But you despise His experience, and the moderation which He exercises toward you.”
Similitude 10.8: I said to him, “Ask Him, lord, whether from the time that He has entered my house I have done anything improper, or have offended Him in any respect.”
Similitude 10.9: He answered, “I also know that you neither have done nor will do anything improper, and therefore I speak these words to you, that you may persevere. For He had a good report of you to me, and you will say these words to others, that they also who have either converted or will still convert may entertain the same feelings with you, and He may report well of these to me, and I to the Lord.”
Similitude 10.10: And I said, “Lord, I make known to every man the great works of God: and I hope that all those who love them, and have sinned before, on hearing these words, may convert, and receive life again.”
Similitude 10.11: “Therefore, continue in this ministry, and finish it. And all who follow out His commands will have life, and great honor with the Lord.
Similitude 10.12: But those who do not keep His commandments, flee from His life, and despise Him. But He has His own honor with the Lord.
Similitude 10.13: All, therefore, who will despise Him, and not follow His commands, deliver themselves to death, and every one of them will be guilty of his own blood.
Similitude 10.14: But I enjoin you, that you obey His commands, and you will have a cure for your former sins.
Similitude 10.15: Moreover, I sent you these virgins, that they may dwell with you. For I saw that they were courteous to you. You will therefore have them as assistants, that you may be the better able to keep His commands: for it is impossible that these commandments can be observed without these virgins.
Similitude 10.16: I see, moreover, that they abide with you willingly; but I will also instruct them not to depart at all from your house: only, keep your house pure, as they will delight to dwell in a pure abode.
Similitude 10.17: For they are pure, and chaste, and industrious, and have all influence with the Lord. Therefore, if they find your house to be pure, they will remain with you; but if any defilement, even a little, befalls it, they will immediately withdraw from your house. For these virgins do not like any defilement at all.”
Similitude 10.18: I said to him, “I hope, lord, that I will please them, so that they may always be willing to inhabit my house. And as He to whom you entrusted me has no complaint against me, so neither will they have.”
Similitude 10.19: He said to the Shepherd, “I see that the servant of God wishes to live, and to keep these commandments, and will place these virgins in a pure habitation.”
Similitude 10.20: When he had spoken these words, he again delivered me to the Shepherd, and called those virgins, and said to them, “Since I see that you are willing to dwell in his house, I commend him and his house to You, asking that You do not withdraw from it at all.” And the virgins heard these words with pleasure.
Similitude 10.21: The messenger then said to me, “Conduct yourself courageously in this service, and make known to everyone the great things of God, and you will have favor in this ministry.
Similitude 10.22: Whoever, therefore, will walk in these commandments, will have life, and will be happy in his life; but whosoever will neglect them will not have life, and will be unhappy in this life. Enjoin all, who are able to act rightly, not to cease well-doing; for, to practice good works is useful to them.
Similitude 10.23: And I say that every man ought to be saved from inconveniences. For both he who is in want, and he who suffers inconveniences in his daily life, is in great torture and necessity. Whoever, therefore, rescues a soul of this kind from necessity, will gain for himself great joy.
Similitude 10.24: For he who is harassed by inconveniences of this kind, suffers equal torture with him who is in chains.
Similitude 10.25: Moreover many, on account of calamities of this sort, when they could not endure them, hasten their own deaths. Whoever, then, knows a calamity of this kind afflicting a man, and does not save him, commits a great sin, and becomes guilty of his blood.
Similitude 10.26: Therefore, do good works, you who have received good from the Lord, lest, while you delay to do them, the building of the tower is finished, and you are rejected from the edifice: there is now no other tower being built.
Similitude 10.27: For on your account the work of building was suspended. Unless, then, you make haste to do rightly, the tower will be completed, and you will be excluded.”
Similitude 10.28: After he had spoken with me, he rose up from the couch, and taking the Shepherd and the virgins, he departed. But he said to me that he would send the Shepherd and the virgins back to my dwelling. Amen.

1.1: The Lord is on my head like a crown, || A nd I will not be without Him.
1.2: They wove for me a crown of truth, || A nd it caused Your branches to bud in me.
1.3: For it is not like a withered crown which does not bud, || B ut You live on my head, || A nd You have blossomed on my head.
1.4: Your fruits are full-grown and perfect, || T hey are full of Your salvation.

3.1: ... I put on.
3.2: And His members are with Him. And I stand on them, || A nd He loves me:
3.3: For I should not have known love, || I f the Lord had not loved me.
3.4: For who is able to distinguish love except the one that is loved?
3.5: I love the Beloved and my soul loves Him:
3.6: And where His rest is, || T here I am also;
3.7: And I will be no stranger, for with the Lord Most High and Merciful there is no grudging.
3.8: I have been united to Him, || F or the lover has found the Beloved,
3.9: And because I will love Him that is the Son, || I will become a son;
3.10: For he that is joined to Him that is immortal, || W ill also himself become immortal;
3.11: And he who has pleasure in the Living One, || W ill become living.
3.12: This is the Spirit of the Lord, || W hich does not lie, || W hich teaches the sons of men to know His ways.
3.13: Be wise and understanding and vigilant. Hallelujah!

4.1: No man, O my God, changes Your holy place;
4.2: And it is not [possible] that he should change it and put it in another place, || B ecause he has no power over it,
4.3: For You have designed Your sanctuary before You made [other] places:
4.4: That which is the elder will not be altered by those that are younger than itself.
4.5: You have given Your heart, O Lord, to Your believers: You will never fail, || N or be without fruits,
4.6: For one hour of Your faith is days and years.
4.7: For who is there [that] put on Your grace, and is hurt?
4.8: For Your seal is known, || A nd Your creatures know it, || A nd Your [heavenly] hosts possess it, || A nd the chosen chief-messengers are clad with it.
4.9: You have given us Your fellowship. I t was not that You were in need of us, || B ut that we are in need of You;
4.10: Distill Your dews on us and open Your rich fountains that pour forth to us milk and honey,
4.11: For there is no relenting with You that You should relent of anything that You have promised.
4.12: And the end was revealed before You, || F or what You gave, You gave freely,
4.13: So that You may not draw them back and take them again,
4.14: For all was revealed before You as God, || A nd ordered from the beginning before You; A nd You, O God, have made all things. Hallelujah!

5.1: I will give thanks to You, O Lord, || B ecause I love You;
5.2: O Most High, You will not forsake me for You are my hope:
5.3: Freely I have received Your grace, || I will live thereby:
5.4: My persecutors will come and not see me:
5.5: A cloud of darkness will fall on their eyes; A nd an air of thick gloom will darken them:
5.6: And they will have no light to see; T hey may not take hold on me.
5.7: Let their counsel become thick darkness, || A nd what they have cunningly devised, || L et it return on their own heads,
5.8: For they have devised a counsel and it did not succeed;
5.9: For my hope is on the Lord and I will not fear, || A nd because the Lord is my salvation, || I will not fear; A nd He is a garland on my head and I will not be moved; E ven if everything should be shaken, || I stand firm;
5.11: And if all things visible should perish, || I will not die, || B ecause the Lord is with me and I am with Him. Hallelujah!

6.1: As the hand moves over the harp, || A nd the strings speak,
6.2: So speaks in my members the Spirit of the Lord, || A nd I speak by His love.
6.3: For it destroys what is foreign and everything that is bitter:
6.4: For thus it was from the beginning and will be to the end, || T hat nothing should be His adversary, || A nd nothing should stand up against Him.
6.5: The Lord has multiplied the knowledge of Himself, || A nd is zealous that these things should be known, || W hich by His grace have been given to us.
6.6: And the praise of His Name He gave us: O ur spirits praise His holy Spirit.
6.7: For there went forth a stream that became a river great and broad;
6.8: For it flooded and broke up everything and it brought [water] to the temple;
6.9: And the restrainers of the children of men were not able to restrain it, || N or the arts of those whose business it is to restrain waters;
6.10: For it spread over the face of the whole earth, || A nd filled everything: A nd all the thirsty on earth were given to drink of it;
6.11: And thirst was relieved and quenched, || F or from the Most High the drink was given.
6.12: Blessed then are the ministers of that drink who are entrusted with that water
6.13: They have assuaged the dry lips, || A nd the will that had fainted they have raised up;
6.14: And souls that were near departing they have caught back from death;
6.15: And limbs that had fallen they straightened and set up.
6.16: They gave strength for their feebleness and light to their eyes,
6.17: For everyone knew them in the Lord, || A nd they lived by the water of life forever. Hallelujah!

7.1: As the impulse of anger against evil, || S o is the impulse of joy over what is lovely, || A nd brings in of its fruits without restraint:
7.2: My joy is the Lord and my impulse is toward Him; T his path of mine is excellent,
7.3: For I have a helper—the Lord.
7.4: He has caused me to know Himself, || W ithout grudging, by His simplicity: His kindness has humbled His greatness.
7.5: He became like me, || I n order that I might receive Him; He was reckoned like myself in order that I might put Him on;
7.7: And I did not tremble when I saw Him, || B ecause He was gracious to me.
7.8: He became like my nature that I might learn Him, || And like my form, that I might not turn back from Him.
7.9: The Father of knowledge is the word of knowledge:
7.10: He who created wisdom is wiser than His works;
7.11: And He who created me when I was yet not knew what I should do when I came into being,
7.12: For what reason He pitied me in His abundant grace, || A nd granted me to ask from Him and to receive from His sacrifice,
7.13: Because it is He that is incorruptible— T he fullness of the ages and their Father.
7.14: He has given Him to be seen of them that are His, || I n order that they may recognize Him that made them, || A nd that they might not suppose that they came of themselves;
7.15: For He has appointed knowledge as its way, || Has widened it and extended it, || A nd brought to all perfection,
7.16: And set over it the traces of His light, || A nd I walked therein from the beginning even to the end.
7.17: For by Him it was worked, || A nd He was resting in the Son, || A nd for its salvation He will take hold of everything.
7.18: And the Most High will be known in His holy ones, || T o announce to those that have songs of the coming of the Lord:
7.19: That they may go forth to meet Him, || A nd may sing to Him with joy and with the harp of many tones.
7.20: The seers will come before Him and they will be seen before Him,
7.21: And they will praise the Lord for His love, || B ecause He is near and beholds.
7.22: And hatred will be taken from the earth, || A nd along with jealousy it will be drowned:
7.23: For ignorance has been destroyed, || B ecause the knowledge of the Lord has arrived.
7.24: They who make songs will sing the grace of the Lord Most High;
7.25: And they will bring their songs, || A nd their heart will be like the day, || A nd like the excellent beauty of the Lord their pleasant song;
7.26: And there will neither be anything that breathes without knowledge nor any that is dumb,
7.27: For He has given a mouth to His creation, || T o open the voice of the mouth toward Him, || T o praise Him,
7.28: Confess His power, || A nd show forth His grace. Hallelujah!

8.1: Open, open your hearts to the exultation of the Lord,
8.2: And let your love be multiplied from the heart and even to the lips,
8.3: To bring forth living [and] holy fruit to the Lord, || A nd to talk with watchfulness in His light.
8.4: Rise up, and stand erect, || You who once were brought low:
8.5: Tell forth, you who were in silence, || T hat your mouth has been opened.
8.6: You, therefore, that were despised, be lifted up from now on, || B ecause your righteousness has been exalted.
8.7: For the right hand of the Lord is with you, || A nd He is your helper; 8 And peace was prepared for you, || B efore your war ever was. 9 Hear the word of truth, || A nd receive the knowledge of the Most High.
8.10: Your flesh has not known what I am saying to you: N either have your hearts known what I am showing to you.
8.11: Keep My secret, || You who are kept by it. 1 2 Keep My faith, || You who are kept by it. 1 3 And understand My knowledge, || You who know Me in truth,
8.14: Love Me with affection, || You who love!
8.15: For I do not turn away My face from them that are Mine;
8.16: For I know them and before they came into being I took knowledge of them, || A nd on their faces I set My seal,
8.17: I fashioned their members, || [And] I prepared My own breasts for them, || T hat they might drink My holy milk and live thereby.
8.18: I took pleasure in them and am not ashamed of them,
8.19: For they are My workmanship and the strength of My thoughts.
8.20: Who then will rise up against My handiwork, || O r who is there that is not subject to them?
8.21: I willed and fashioned mind and heart: A nd they are Mine, and by My own right hand I set My chosen ones,
8.22: And My righteousness goes before them and they will not be deprived of My Name, || F or it is with them.
8.23: Ask, and abound, and abide in the love of the Lord,
8.24: You beloved ones in the Beloved— T hose who are kept in Him that lives,
8.25: And they that are saved in Him that was saved—
8.26: And you will be found incorruptible in all ages through the Name of your Father. Hallelujah!

9.1: Open your ears and I will speak to you. Give me your souls that I may also give you my soul,
9.2: The word of the Lord and His good pleasures, || T he holy thought which He has devised concerning His Messiah.
9.3: For in the will of the Lord is your salvation, || A nd His thought is everlasting life; A nd your end is immortality.
9.4: Be enriched in God the Father, || A nd receive the thought of the Most High.
9.5: Be strong and be redeemed by His grace.
9.6: That none of those who hear may fall in war, || A nd that those again who have known Him may not perish, || A nd that those who receive may not be ashamed.
9.8: Truth is an everlasting crown forever. Blessed are they who set it on their heads:
9.9: It is a stone of great price; A nd there have been wars on account of the crown.
9.10: And righteousness has taken it and has given it to you.
9.11: Put on the crown in the true covenant of the Lord.
9.12: And all those who have conquered will be written in His scroll.
9.13: For their scroll is victory which is yours. And she [(Victory)] sees you before her and wills that you will be saved. Hallelujah!

10.1: The Lord has directed My mouth by His word, || A nd He has opened My heart by His light, || A nd He has caused His deathless life to dwell in Me,
10.2: And He gave Me that I might speak the fruit of peace:
10.3: To convert the souls of them who are willing to come to Him, || A nd to lead captive a good captivity for freedom.
10.4: I was strengthened and made mighty and took the world captive;
10.5: And it became to Me for the praise of the Most High, || A nd of God My Father.
10.6: And the nations were gathered together who were scattered abroad.
10.7: And I was unpolluted by My love for them, || B ecause they confessed Me in high places: A nd the traces of the light were set on their heart,
10.8: And they walked in My life and were saved and became My people forever and ever. Hallelujah!

11.1: My heart was split, || A nd its flower appeared; A nd grace sprang up in it: A nd it brought forth fruit to the Lord,
11.2: For the Most High cleaved my heart by His Holy Spirit and searched my affection toward Him: A nd filled me with His love.
11.3: And His opening of me became my salvation; A nd I ran in His way in His peace even in the way of truth:
11.4: from the beginning and even to the end I acquired His knowledge,
11.5: And I was established on the rock of truth, || W here He had set me up;
11.6: And speaking waters touched my lips from the fountain of the Lord plenteously,
11.7: And I drank and was inebriated with the living water that does not die;
11.8: And my inebriation was not one without knowledge, || B ut I forsook vanity and turned to my God, the Most High,
11.9: And I was enriched by His bounty, || A nd I forsook the folly which is diffused over the earth; A nd I stripped it off and cast it from me:
11.10: And the Lord renewed me in His raiment, || A nd possessed me by His light, || A nd from above He gave me rest in incorruption;
11.11: And I became like the land which blossoms and rejoices in its fruits:
11.12: And the Lord was like the sun shining on the face of the land;
11.13: He lightened my eyes and my face received the dew— T he pleasant fragrance of the Lord;
11.14: And He carried me to His Paradise, || W here the abundance of the pleasure of the Lord is;
11.15: And I worshiped the Lord on account of His glory; A nd I said, “Blessed, O Lord, are they who are planted in Your land, || And those who have a place in Your Paradise;
11.16: And they grow by the fruits of the trees. And they have changed from darkness to light.
11.17: Behold! A ll Your servants are fair, who do good works, || A nd turn away from wickedness to the pleasantness that is Yours:
11.18: And they have turned back the bitterness of the trees from them, || W hen they were planted in Your land;
11.19: And everything became like a relic of Yourself, || A nd memorial forever of Your faithful works.
11.20: For there is abundant room in Your Paradise, || A nd nothing is useless therein;
11.21: But everything is filled with fruit; G lory be to You, O God, the Delight of Paradise forever. Hallelujah!”

12.1: He has filled me with words of truth, || T hat I may speak the same;
12.2: And like the flow of waters flows truth from my mouth, || A nd my lips show forth His fruit.
12.3: And He has caused His knowledge to abound in me, || B ecause the mouth of the Lord is the true Word, || A nd the door of His light;
12.4: And the Most High has given Him to His generations, || W hich are the interpreters of His own beauty, || A nd the repeaters of His praise, || A nd the confessors of His counsel, || And the heralds of His thought, || And the chasteners of His servants.
12.5: For the swiftness of the Word is inexpressible, || A nd like His expression is His swiftness and force;
12.6: And His course knows no limit. He never fails, but He stands sure, || A nd He does not know descent nor the way of it.
12.7: For as His work is, so is His end, || F or He is light and the dawning of thought;
12.8: And by Him the worlds talk to one another; A nd in the Word there were those that were silent;
12.9: And from Him came love and concord; A nd they spoke to one another whatever was theirs; A nd they were penetrated by the Word;
12.10: And they knew Him who made them, || B ecause they were in concord; F or the mouth of the Most High spoke to them; A nd His explanation ran by means of Him.
12.11: For the dwelling-place of the Word is man, || A nd His truth is love.
12.12: Blessed are they who by means thereof have understood everything, || A nd have known the Lord in His truth. Hallelujah!

13.1: Behold! T he Lord is our mirror: O pen the eyes and see them in Him, || A nd learn the manner of your face,
13.2: And tell forth praise to His Spirit, || A nd wipe off the filth from your face, || A nd love His holiness, || A nd clothe yourselves with it,
13.3: And be without stain at all times before Him. Hallelujah!

14.1: As the eyes of a son to his father, || So are my eyes, O Lord, at all times toward You.
14.2: For with You are my consolations and my delight.
14.3: Do not turn Your mercies away from me, O Lord: A nd do not take Your kindness from me.
14.4: Stretch out to me, O Lord, at all times Your right hand, || A nd be my guide even to the end, || A ccording to Your good pleasure.
14.5: Let me be well-pleasing before You, || B ecause of Your glory and because of Your Name.
14.6: Let me be preserved from evil, || A nd let Your meekness, O Lord, || A bide with me, and the fruits of Your love.
14.7: Teach me the psalms of Your truth, || T hat I may bring forth fruit in You,
14.8: And open to me the harp of Your Holy Spirit, || T hat with all His notes I may praise You, O Lord.
14.9: And according to the multitude of Your tender mercies, || S o You will give to me; A nd hasten to grant our petitions; A nd You are able for all our needs. Hallelujah!

15.1: As the sun is the joy to them that seek for its daybreak, || S o my joy is the Lord,
15.2: Because He is my Sun, and His rays have lifted me up, and His light has dispelled all darkness from my face. In Him I have acquired eyes and have seen His holy day:
15.4: Ears have become mine and I have heard His truth.
15.5: The thought of knowledge has been mine, || A nd I have been delighted through Him.
15.6: I have left the way of error, || A nd have walked toward Him, || And have received salvation from Him without grudging.
15.7: And according to His bounty He has given to me, || And according to His excellent beauty He has made me.
15.8: I have put on incorruption through His Name, || A nd have put off corruption by His grace.
15.9: Death has been destroyed before my face, || A nd Sheol has been abolished by my word;
15.10: And there has gone up deathless life in the Lord ’s land,
15.11: And it has been made known to His faithful ones, || A nd has been given without limit to all those that trust in Him. Hallelujah!

16.1: As the work of the farmer is the plowshare, || A nd the work of the helmsman is the guidance of the ship,
16.2: So also my work, my craft, is the psalm of the Lord,
16.3: Because His love has nourished my heart, || A nd even to my lips He poured out His fruits.
16.4: For my love is the Lord, || A nd I will therefore sing to Him:
16.5: For I am made strong in His praise, || A nd I have faith in Him.
16.6: I will open my mouth and His Spirit will utter in me || The glory of the Lord and His beauty, || T he work of His hands and the operation of His fingers,
16.7: The multitude of His mercies and the strength of His word.
16.8: For the Word of the Lord searches out all things, || B oth the invisible and that which reveals His thought,
16.9: For the eye sees His works and the ear hears His thought.
16.10: He spread out the earth and settled the waters in the sea;
16.11: He measured the heavens and fixed the stars; A nd He established the creation and set it up;
16.12: And He rested from His works.
16.13: Thus created things run in their courses, || A nd do their works:
16.14: And they do not know how to stand and be idle; A nd His heavenly hosts are subject to His Word.
16.15: The treasure-chamber of the light is the sun, || A nd the treasury of the darkness is the night;
16.16: And He made the sun for the day that it may be bright, || B ut night brings darkness over the face of the land;
16.17: And their cycles, one after another, speak the beauty of God.
16.18: And there is nothing that is without the Lord, F or He was before anything came into being,
16.19: And the worlds were made by His word, || A nd by the thought of His heart. Glory and honor to His Name. Hallelujah!

17.1: I was crowned by My God —M y crown is living;
17.2: And I was justified in My Lord —M y incorruptible salvation is He.
17.3: I was loosed from vanity, || A nd I was not condemned;
17.4: The choking bonds were cut off by her hands. I received the face and the fashion of a new person, || A nd I walked in it and was delivered.
17.5: And the thought of truth led me on; And I walked after it and did not wander.
17.6: And all that have seen Me were amazed: A nd I was regarded by them as a strange person.
17.7: And He who knew and brought Me up is the Most High in all His perfection. And He glorified Me by His kindness, || A nd raised My thoughts to the height of His truth.
17.8: And from there He gave Me the way of His precepts, || And I opened the doors that were closed,
17.9: And broke in pieces the bars of iron, || B ut my iron melted and dissolved before Me;
17.10: Nothing appeared closed to Me, || B ecause I was the door of everything.
17.11: And I went over to all My bound ones to loose them, || T hat I might not leave any man bound or binding.
17.12: And I imparted My knowledge without grudging, || A nd My prayer was in My love;
17.13: And I sowed My fruits in hearts, || A nd transformed them into Myself; A nd they received My blessing and lived;
17.14: And they were gathered to Me and were saved, || B ecause they were to Me as My own members, and I was their Head. Glory to You, our Head, the Lord Messiah. Hallelujah!

18.1: My heart was lifted up in the love of the Most High and was enlarged, || T hat I might praise Him for His Name’s sake.
18.2: My members were strengthened that they might not fall from His strength.
18.3: Sicknesses departed from my body, || A nd it stood to the Lord by His will, || For His kingdom is true.
18.4: O Lord, for the sake of them that are deficient, do not remove Your word from me!
18.5: Neither for the sake of their works do You restrain from me Your perfection!
18.6: Do not let the luminary be conquered by the darkness, || N or let truth flee away from falsehood.
18.7: You will appoint me to victory; Your right hand is our salvation. And You will receive men from all quarters.
18.8: And You will preserve whosoever is held in evils:
18.9: You are my God. Falsehood and death are not in Your mouth,
18.10: For Your will is perfection, || A nd You do not know vanity,
18.12: And You do not know error,
18.14: And ignorance appeared like a blind man, || A nd like the foam of the sea,
18.15: And they supposed of that vain thing that it was something great;
18.16: And they, too, came in likeness of it and became vain; A nd those have understood who have known and meditated;
18.17: And they have not been corrupt in their imagination, || F or such were in the mind of the Lord;
18.18: And they mocked at them that were walking in error;
18.19: And they spoke truth from the inspiration which the Most High breathed into them. Praise and great honor to His Name. Hallelujah!

19.1: A cup of milk was offered to me, || A nd I drank it in the sweetness of the delight of the Lord.
19.2: The Son is the cup and He who was milked is the Father:
19.3: And the Holy Spirit milked Him, || B ecause His breasts were full, || A nd it was necessary for Him that His milk should be sufficiently released;
19.4: And the Holy Spirit opened His bosom and mingled the milk from the two breasts of the Father and gave the mixture to the world without their knowing:
19.5: And they who receive in its fullness are the ones on the right hand.
19.6: The Spirit opened the womb of the virgin and she received conception and brought forth; A nd the virgin became a mother with many mercies;
19.7: And she travailed and brought forth a Son without incurring pain;
19.8: And because she was not sufficiently prepared, || A nd she had not sought a midwife (for He brought her to bear) she brought forth, || A s if she were a man, of her own will;
19.9: And she brought Him forth openly, || A nd acquired Him with great dignity,
19.10: And loved Him in His swaddling clothes and guarded Him kindly, || A nd showed Him in Majesty. Hallelujah!

20.1: I am a priest of the Lord, || A nd to Him I do priestly service: A nd to Him I offer the sacrifice of His thought.
20.2: For His thought is not like the thought of the world nor the thought of the flesh, || N or like them that serve carnally.
20.3: The sacrifice of the Lord is righteousness, || A nd purity of heart and lips.
20.4: Present your reins before Him blamelessly, || A nd do not let your heart do violence to heart, || N or your soul to soul.
20.5: You will not acquire a stranger by the price of your silver, || Neither will you seek to devour your neighbor,
20.6: Neither will you deprive him of the covering of his nakedness.
20.7: But put on the grace of the Lord without limit; A nd come into His Paradise and make a garland from its tree;
20.8: And put it on your head and be glad; A nd recline on His rest, || A nd glory will go before you,
20.9: And you will receive of His kindness and of His grace; A nd you will be flourishing in truth in the praise of His holiness. Praise and honor be to His Name. Hallelujah!

21.1: I lifted up my arms to the Most High, || E ven to the grace of the Lord, || B ecause He had cast off my bonds from me, || A nd my Helper had lifted me up to His grace and to His salvation.
21.2: And I put off darkness and clothed myself with light,
21.3: And my soul acquired a body free from sorrow, or affliction, or pains.
21.4: And increasingly helpful to me was the thought of the Lord, || A nd His fellowship in incorruption:
21.5: And I was lifted up in His light; A nd I served before Him,
21.6: And I became near to Him, || P raising and confessing Him;
21.7: My heart ran over and was found in my mouth, || A nd it arose on my lips, || A nd the exultation of the Lord increased on my face, || A nd His praise likewise. Hallelujah!

22.1: He who brought Me down from on high, || A lso brought Me up from the regions below;
22.2: And He who gathers together the things that are in between is He who also cast Me down;
22.3: He who scattered My enemies and My adversaries had existed from ancient [times].
22.4: He who gave Me authority over bonds that I might loose them,
22.5: He that overthrew the dragon with seven heads by My hands, || A nd set Me over his roots that I might destroy his seed—
22.6: You were there and helped Me, || A nd in every place Your Name was a rampart to Me.
22.7: Your right hand destroyed his wicked poison; A nd Your hand leveled the way for those who believe in You.
22.8: And You chose them from the graves and separated them from the dead.
22.9: You took dead bones and covered them with bodies.
22.10: They were motionless, || A nd You gave them energy for life.
22.11: Your way and Your face were without corruption; Y ou brought Your world to corruption, || T hat everything might be dissolved, and then renewed,
22.12: And that the foundation for everything might be Your rock: A nd on it You built Your Kingdom; A nd it became the dwelling-place of the holy ones. Hallelujah!

23.1: Joy is of the holy ones! A nd who will put it on, but they alone?
23.2: Grace is of the chosen ones! A nd who will receive it except those who trust in it from the beginning?
23.3: Love is of the chosen ones! And who will put it on except those who have possessed it from the beginning?
23.4: Walk in the knowledge of the Most High without grudging: T o His exultation and to the perfection of His knowledge.
23.5: And His thought was like a letter; His will descended from on high, || A nd it was sent like an arrow which is violently [shot] from the bow:
23.6: And many hands rushed to the letter to seize it and to take and read it,
23.7: And it escaped their fingers, and they were frightened at it and at the seal that was on it,
23.8: Because it was not permitted to them to loose its seal, || F or the power that was over the seal was greater than they.
23.9: But those who saw it went after the letter that they might know where it would descend, || A nd who should read it and who should hear it.
23.10: But a wheel received it and came over it:
23.11: And there was with it a sign of the Kingdom and of the government,
23.12: And everything which tried to move the wheel it mowed and cut down.
23.13: And it gathered the multitude of adversaries, || A nd bridged the rivers and crossed over, and rooted up many forests and made a broad path.
23.14: The head went down to the feet, for the wheel ran down to the feet, || A nd that which was a sign on it.
23.15: The letter was one of command, || F or there were included in it all districts;
23.16: And there was seen at its head, || T he Head which was revealed—even the Son of Truth from the Most High Father,
23.17: And He inherited and took possession of everything. And the thought of many was brought to nothing.
23.18: And all the apostates hastened and fled away. And those who persecuted and were enraged became extinct,
23.19: And the letter was a great volume, || W hich was wholly written by the finger of God:
23.20: And the Name of the Father was on it, and of the Son and of the Holy Spirit, || T o rule forever and ever. Hallelujah!

24.1: The Dove fluttered over the Messiah, || B ecause He was her head; A nd she sang over Him and her voice was heard,
24.2: And the inhabitants were afraid, and the sojourners were moved,
24.3: The birds dropped their wings, and all creeping things died in their holes; A nd the abysses were opened which had been hidden, || A nd they cried to the Lord like women in travail:
24.4: And no food was given to them, || B ecause it did not belong to them;
24.5: And they sealed up the abysses with the seal of the Lord, || And they perished in the thought [of] those that had existed from ancient times,
24.6: For they were corrupt from the beginning; A nd the end of their corruption was life:
24.7: And each of them that was imperfect perished, || F or it was not possible to give them a word that they might remain,
24.8: And the Lord destroyed the imaginations of all them that did not have the truth with them.
24.9: For they who were lifted up in their hearts were deficient in wisdom, and so they were rejected, || B ecause the truth was not with them.
24.10: For the Lord disclosed His way and spread His grace abroad: A nd those who understood it know His holiness. Hallelujah!

25.1: I was rescued from my bonds and to You, my God, I fled,
25.2: For You are the right hand of my salvation and my helper.
25.3: You have restrained those that rise up against me,
25.4: And I will see him no longer, || B ecause Your face was with me, || W hich saved me by Your grace.
25.5: But I was despised and rejected in the eyes of many, || A nd I was like lead in their eyes,
25.6: And strength was mine from Yourself and [Your] help.
25.7: You set a lamp at my right hand and at my left, || A nd in me there will be nothing that is not bright.
25.8: And I was clothed with the covering of Your Spirit, || A nd You removed my raiment of skin from me;
25.9: For Your right hand lifted me up and removed sickness from me,
25.10: And I became mighty in the truth, || A nd holy by Your righteousness; A nd all my adversaries were afraid of me.
25.11: And I became admirable by the Name of the Lord, || A nd I was justified by His gentleness, || A nd His rest is forever and ever. Hallelujah!

26.1: I poured out praise to the Lord, for I am His:
26.2: And I will speak His holy song for my heart is with Him.
26.3: For His harp is in my hands, || A nd the odes of His rest will not be silent.
26.4: I will cry to Him from my whole heart; I will praise and exalt Him with all my members.
26.5: For from the east and even to the west is His praise;
26.6: And from the south and even to the north is the confession of Him;
26.7: And from the top of the hills to their utmost bound is His perfection.
26.8: Who can write the psalms of the Lord, || O r who read them?
26.9: Or who can train his soul for life that his soul may be saved?
26.10: Or who can press on the Most High, || S o that with His mouth He may speak?
26.11: Who is able to interpret the wonders of the Lord?
26.12: For he who could interpret would be dissolved and would become that which is interpreted.
26.13: For it suffices to know and to rest, || F or in rest the singers stand,
26.14: Like a river which has an abundant fountain, || A nd flows to the help of them that seek it. Hallelujah!

27.1: I stretched out my hands and sanctified my Lord,
27.2: For the extension of my hands is His sign:
27.3: And my expansion is the upright tree [[ or cross ]].

28.1: As the wings of doves over their nestlings, || A nd the mouth of their nestlings toward their mouths,
28.2: So are also the wings of the Spirit over my heart.
28.3: My heart is delighted and exults || L ike the baby who exults in the womb of his mother.
28.4: I believed, therefore I was at rest, || F or He in whom I have believed is faithful.
28.5: He has richly blessed me, and my head is with Him, || A nd the sword will not divide me from Him, || N or the scimitar,
28.6: For I am ready before destruction comes; A nd I have been set on His immortal pinions.
28.7: And He showed me His sign: [Poured] forth and given me to drink, || A nd from that life is the spirit within me and it cannot die, for it lives.
28.8: They who saw me marveled at me, || B ecause I was persecuted, || A nd they supposed that I was swallowed up, || F or I seemed to them as one of the lost;
28.9: And my oppression became my salvation; A nd I was their reprobation because there was no seal in me.
28.10: I was hated because I did good to every man,
28.11: And they came around me like mad dogs || W ho ignorantly attack their masters,
28.12: For their thought is corrupt and their understanding perverted.
28.13: But I was carrying water in my right hand and their bitterness I endured by my sweetness:
28.14: And I did not perish, || F or I was not their brother nor was my birth like theirs.
28.15: And they sought for my death and did not find it, || F or I was older than the memorial of them;
28.16: And vainly did they make attack on me, || And those who, without reward, came after me:
28.17: They sought to destroy the memorial of Him who was before them.
28.18: For the thought of the Most High cannot be anticipated; A nd His heart is superior to all wisdom. Hallelujah!

29.1: The Lord is my hope: I n Him I will not be confounded.
29.2: For according to His praise He made me, || A nd according to His goodness even so He gave to me,
29.3: And according to His mercies He exalted me, || A nd according to His excellent beauty He set me on high,
29.4: And brought me up out of the depths of Sheol, || A nd from the mouth of death He drew me.
29.5: And You laid my enemies low, || And He justified me by His grace.
29.6: For I believed in the Lord ’s Messiah: A nd it appeared to me that He is the Lord.
29.7: And He showed me His sign, || A nd He led me by His light, || A nd gave me the rod of His power
29.8: That I might subdue the imaginations of the peoples, || A nd the power of the men of might, to bring them low,
29.9: To make war by His word, || A nd to take victory by His power.
29.10: And the Lord overthrew my enemy by His word: A nd he became like the stubble which the wind carries away;
29.11: And I gave praise to the Most High because He exalted me—His servant and the son of His handmaid. Hallelujah!

30.1: Fill waters for yourselves from the living fountain of the Lord, || F or it is opened to you:
30.2: And come all you thirsty and take the drink; A nd rest by the fountain of the Lord.
30.3: For it is fair and pure and gives rest to the soul. Its waters are much more pleasant than honey;
30.4: And the honeycomb of bees is not to be compared with it.
30.5: For it flows forth from the lips of the Lord, || And from the heart of the Lord is its name.
30.6: And it came infinitely and invisibly: A nd until it was set in the midst, they did not know it.
30.7: Blessed are they who have drunk from that and have found rest thereby. Hallelujah!

31.1: The abysses were dissolved before the Lord, || A nd darkness was destroyed by His appearance.
31.2: Error went astray and perished at His hand, || A nd folly found no path to walk in, || A nd was submerged by the truth of the Lord.
31.3: He opened His mouth and spoke grace and joy: A nd He spoke a new song of praise to His Name.
31.4: And He lifted up His voice to the Most High, || And offered the sons that were with Him.
31.5: And His face was justified, || F or thus His holy Father had given to Him.
31.6: Come forth, you that have been afflicted, and receive joy, || A nd possess your souls by His grace; A nd take immortal life to yourself.
31.7: And they made Me a debtor when I rose up— M e who had been a debtor: A nd they divided My spoil, || T hough nothing was due to them.
31.8: But I endured, and held My peace, and was silent as if not moved by them.
31.9: But I stood unshaken like a firm rock which is beaten by the waves and endures.
31.10: And I bore their bitterness for humility’s sake,
31.11: In order that I might redeem My people and inherit it, || A nd that I might not make void My promises || To the fathers to whom I promised the salvation of their seed. Hallelujah!

32.1: To the blessed there is joy from their hearts, || A nd light from Him that dwells in them,
32.2: And words from the Truth, who was self-existent; F or He is strengthened by the holy power of the Most High: A nd He is unmoved forever and ever. Hallelujah!

33.1: Again Grace ran and renounced the corruptor, || A nd came down on him to bring him to nothing—
33.2: And he who caused destruction [and] perdition from before him, || A nd devastated all his order.
33.3: And he stood on a lofty summit || And uttered his voice from one end of the earth to the other,
33.4: And drew to himself all those who obeyed him, || For he did not appear as [the] evil one.
33.5: But there stood a perfect virgin who was proclaiming, and calling, and saying,
33.6: “O you sons of men, return! A nd you daughters of men, come!
33.7: And forsake the ways of that corruptor and draw near to me, || A nd I will enter into you, || A nd will bring you forth from perdition,
33.8: And make yourself wise in the ways of truth, || T hat you are not destroyed nor perish.
33.9: Hear me and be redeemed! For I am telling of the Grace of God among you”: A nd by My means you will be redeemed and become blessed.
33.10: I am your Judge; A nd they who have put Me on will not be injured, || B ut they will possess the new world that is incorruptible.
33.11: My chosen ones walk in Me, || A nd I will make known My ways to them that seek Me, || A nd I will make them trust in My Name. Hallelujah!

34.1: No way is hard where there is a simple heart,
34.2: Nor is there any wound where the thoughts are upright,
34.3: Nor is there any storm in the depth of the illuminated thought.
34.4: Where one is surrounded on every side by beauty, || T here is nothing that is divided.
34.5: The likeness of what is below is that which is above, || F or everything is above. W hat is below is nothing but the imagination of those that are without knowledge.
34.6: Grace has been revealed for your salvation. Believe, and live, and be saved. Hallelujah!

35.1: In quietness He distilled the dew of the Lord on me,
35.2: And He caused the cloud of peace to rise over my head, || W hich guarded me continually;
35.3: It was to me for salvation: E verything was shaken, and they were frightened;
35.4: And there came forth from them smoke and judgment; A nd I was keeping quiet in the order of the Lord.
35.5: He was more than a shelter to me and more than a foundation.
35.6: And I was carried like a child by its mother: A nd He gave me milk, || T he dew of the Lord,
35.7: And I grew great by His bounty, || A nd rested in His perfection,
35.8: And I spread out my hands in the lifting up of my soul: A nd I was made right with the Most High, || And I was redeemed with Him. Hallelujah!

36.1: I rested in the Spirit of the Lord, || A nd the Spirit raised Me on high,
36.2: And made Me stand on My feet in the height of the Lord, || B efore His perfection and His glory, || W hile I was praising Him by the composition of His songs.
36.3: The Spirit brought Me forth before the face of the Lord, || A nd, although [the] Son of Man, || I was [also] named the Illuminate, the Son of God,
36.4: While I was praised among the praising ones, || A nd I was great among the mighty ones.
36.5: For according to the greatness of the Most High, || S o He made Me; A nd like His own newness He renewed Me, || A nd He anointed Me from His own perfection.
36.6: And I became one of His neighbors; A nd My mouth was opened like a cloud of dew,
36.7: And my heart poured out as it were a gushing stream of righteousness,
36.8: And my access to Him was in peace. A nd I was established by the Spirit of His government. Hallelujah!

37.1: I stretched out my hands to my Lord, || A nd to the Most High I raised my voice:
37.2: And I spoke with the lips of my heart, || A nd He heard me when my voice reached Him;
37.3: His answer came to me and gave me the fruits of my labors;
37.4: And it gave me rest by the grace of the Lord. Hallelujah!

38.1: I went up to the light of Truth as if into a chariot:
38.2: And the Truth took me and led me, || A nd carried me across pits and ravines; A nd from the rocks and the waves He preserved me.
38.3: And He became to me a haven of salvation, || A nd He set me on the arms of immortal life;
38.4: And He went with me and made me rest, || A nd did not permit me to wander because He was and is the Truth.
38.5: And I ran no risk, || B ecause I walked with Him; 6 And I did not error in anything because I obeyed the Truth.
38.7: For error flees away from Him and does not meet Him, || B ut the Truth proceeds in the right path.
38.8: And whatever I did not know, He made clear to me, || A ll the poisons of error, || A nd the plagues of death which they think to be sweetness.
38.9: And I saw the destroying of the destroyer, || W hen the bride who is corrupted is adorned: A nd the bridegroom who corrupts and is corrupted.
38.10: And I asked the Truth, “Who are these?” A nd He said to me, “This is the deceiver and the error:
38.11: And they are alike in the beloved and in his bride; A nd they lead astray and corrupt the whole world.
38.12: And they invite many to the banquet,
38.13: And give them to drink of the wine of their intoxication, || A nd remove their wisdom and knowledge, || A nd so they make them without intelligence;
38.14: And then they leave them; A nd then these go about like madmen corrupting: S eeing that they are without heart, || N or do they seek for it.”
38.15: And I was made wise so as not to fall into the hands of the deceiver; A nd I congratulated myself because the Truth went with me,
38.16: And I was established, and lived, and was redeemed,
38.17: And my foundations were laid on the hand of the Lord, || B ecause He established me.
38.18: For He set the root, and watered it, and fixed it, and blessed it; A nd its fruits are forever.
38.19: It struck deep, and sprung up, and spread out, and was full and enlarged.
38.20: And the Lord alone was glorified in His planting and in His farming || B y His care and by the blessing of His lips,
38.21: By the beautiful planting of His right hand, || A nd by the discovery of His planting, || A nd by the thought of His mind. Hallelujah!

39.1: Great rivers are the power of the Lord:
39.2: And they carry headlong those who despise Him, || A nd entangle their paths;
39.3: And they sweep away their fords, || A nd catch their bodies, and destroy their lives.
39.4: For they are swifter than lightning and more rapid, || A nd those who cross them in faith are not moved;
39.5: And those who walk on them without blemish will not be afraid.
39.6: For the sign in them is the Lord; A nd the sign is the way of those who cross in the Name of the Lord;
39.7: Therefore put on the Name of the Most High, || A nd know Him and you will cross without danger, || F or the rivers will be subject to you.
39.8: The Lord has bridged them by His word; A nd He walked and crossed them on foot:
39.9: And His footsteps stand firm on the water, || A nd are not injured; T hey are as firm as a tree that is truly set up.
39.10: And the waves were lifted up on this side and on that, || B ut the footsteps of our Lord Messiah stand firm, and are not obliterated, and are not defaced.
39.11: And a way has been appointed for those who cross after Him, and for those who adhere to the course of faith in Him and worship His Name. Hallelujah!

40.1: As the honey distills from the comb of the bees,
40.2: And the milk flows from the woman that loves her children;
40.3: So also is my hope on You, my God.
40.4: As the fountain gushes out its water,
40.5: So my heart gushes out the praise of the Lord, and my lips utter praise to Him, || A nd my tongue His psalms,
40.6: And my face exults with His gladness, and my spirit exults in His love, and my soul shines in Him.
40.7: And reverence confides in Him, || A nd redemption stands assured in Him.
40.8: And His inheritance is immortal life, || A nd those who participate in it are incorruptible. Hallelujah!

41.1: All the Lord ’s children will praise Him, || A nd will collect the truth of His faith.
41.2: And His children will be known to Him. Therefore, we will sing in His love.
41.3: We live in the Lord by His grace, || A nd we receive life in His Messiah.
41.4: For a great day has shined on us: A nd marvelous is He who has given us of His glory.
41.5: Therefore, let all of us unite together in the Name of the Lord, || A nd let us honor Him in His goodness,
41.6: And let our faces shine in His light, || A nd let our hearts meditate in His love by night and by day.
41.7: Let us exult with the joy of the Lord.
41.8: All those that see me will be astonished, || For I am from another race;
41.9: For the Father of truth remembered me—He who possessed me from the beginning.
41.10: For His bounty begot me, || A nd the thought of His heart.
41.11: And His Word is with us in all our ways—
41.12: The Savior who makes alive and does not reject our souls;
41.13: The Man who was humbled, || A nd exalted by His own righteousness.
41.14: The Son of the Most High appeared in the perfection of His Father,
41.15: And light dawned from the Word that was before time in Him.
41.16: The Messiah is truly one; A nd He was known before the foundation of the world,
41.17: That He might save souls forever by the truth of His Name: A new song arises from those who love Him. Hallelujah!

42.1: I stretched out my hands and approached my Lord,
42.2: My expansion is the outspread tree [[ or cross ]], which was set up on the way of the Righteous One.
42.4: And I became of no account to those who did not take hold of Me and I will be with those who love Me.
42.5: All my persecutors are dead; A nd they sought after Me who hoped in Me, || B ecause I was alive.
42.6: And I rose up and am with them; A nd I will speak by their mouths,
42.7: For they have despised those who persecuted them.
42.8: And I lifted up over them the yoke of My love.
42.9: Like the arm of the bridegroom over the bride,
42.10: So was My yoke over those that know Me;
42.11: And as the couch that is spread in the house of the bridegroom and bride,
42.12: So is My love over those that believe in Me.
42.13: Sheol saw Me and was made miserable:
42.14: I did not perish [forever], || T hough they devised it against Me.
42.16: Death cast Me up and many along with Me.
42.17: I had gall and bitterness, || A nd I went down with him to the utmost of his depth:
42.18: And the feet and the head he let go, || F or they were not able to endure My face.
42.19: And I made a congregation of living men among his dead men, || A nd I spoke with them by living lips,
42.20: Because My word will not be void.
42.21: And those who had died ran toward Me, || A nd they cried and said, “Son of God, have pity on us, || A nd do with us according to Your kindness.
42.22: And bring us out from the bonds of darkness, || A nd open to us the door by which we will come out to You.
42.23: For we see that our death has not touched You.
42.24: Let us also be redeemed with You, || F or You are our Redeemer.”
42.25: And I heard their voice, || A nd I sealed My Name on their heads;
42.26: For they are free men, and they are Mine. Hallelujah!

1.1: I, O King, by the grace of God came into this world;
1.2: and when I had considered the heavens, and the earth, and the seas, and had surveyed the sun and the rest of creation,
1.3: I marveled at the beauty of the world.
1.4: And I perceived that the world and all that is therein are moved by the power of another;
1.5: and I understood that He who moves them is God, who is hidden in them, and veiled by them.
1.6: And it is manifest that that which causes motion is more powerful than that which is moved.
1.7: But that I should make search concerning this same Mover of all, as to what is His nature (for it seems to me, He is indeed unsearchable in His nature),
1.8: and that I should argue as to the constancy of His government, so as to grasp it fully—this is a vain effort for me;
1.9: for it is not possible that a man should fully comprehend it.
1.10: I say, however, concerning this Mover of the world, that He is God of all,
1.11: who made all things for the sake of mankind.
1.12: And it seems to me that this is reasonable, that one should fear God and should not oppress man.
1.13: I say, then, that God is not born, not made, an ever-abiding nature without beginning and without end, immortal, perfect, and incomprehensible.
1.14: Now when I say that He is “perfect,” this means that there is not in Him any defect,
1.15: and He is not in need of anything but all things are in need of Him.
1.16: And when I say that He is “without beginning,” this means that everything which has beginning has also an end, and that which has an end may be brought to an end.
1.17: He has no name, for everything which has a name is related to things created.
1.18: He has no form, nor yet any union of members; for whatever possesses these is related to things fashioned.
1.19: He is neither male nor female.
1.20: The heavens do not limit Him, but the heavens and all things, visible and invisible, receive their bounds from Him.
1.21: He has no adversary, for there exists not any stronger than He.
1.22: Wrath and indignation He does not possess, for there is nothing which is able to stand against Him.
1.23: Ignorance and forgetfulness are not in His nature, for He is altogether wisdom and understanding;
1.24: and in Him stands fast all that exists.
1.25: He requires not sacrifice and libation, nor even one of things visible;
1.26: He requires not anything from any, but all living creatures stand in need of Him.

2.1: Since, then, we have addressed you concerning God, so far as our discourse can bear on Him,
2.2: let us now come to the race of men, that we may know which of them participate in the truth of which we have spoken, and which of them go astray from it.
2.3: This is clear to you, O King, that there are four classes of men in this world: Barbarians and Greeks, Jews and Christians.
2.4: The Barbarians, indeed, trace the origin of their kind of religion from Kronos and from Rhea and their other gods;
2.5: the Greeks, however, from Helenos, who is said to be sprung from Zeus.
2.6: And by Helenos there were born Aiolos and Xuthos;
2.7: and there were others descended from Inachos and Phoroneus,
2.8: and lastly from the Egyptian Danaos and from Kadmos and from Dionysos.
2.9: The Jews, again, trace the origin of their race from Abraham, who begot Isaac, of whom was born Jacob.
2.10: And he begot twelve sons who migrated from Syria to Egypt;
2.11: and there they were called the nation of the Hebrews, by him who made their laws;
2.12: and at length they were named Jews.
2.13: The Christians, then, trace the beginning of their religion from Jesus the Messiah;
2.14: and He is named the Son of God Most High.
2.15: And it is said that God came down from Heaven,
2.16: and from a Hebrew virgin assumed and clothed Himself with flesh;
2.17: and the Son of God lived in a daughter of man.
2.18: This is taught in the Gospel, as it is called, which a short time was preached among them;
2.19: and you also if you will read therein, may perceive the power which belongs to it.
2.20: This Jesus, then, was born of the race of the Hebrews;
2.21: and He had twelve disciples in order that the purpose of His incarnation might in time be accomplished.
2.22: But He Himself was pierced by the Jews, and He died and was buried;
2.23: and they say that after three days He rose and [later] ascended to Heaven.
2.24: Thereon these twelve disciples went forth throughout the known parts of the world and kept showing His greatness with all modesty and uprightness.
2.25: And hence also those of the present day who believe that preaching are called Christians, and they have become famous.
2.26: So then there are, as I said above, four classes of men: Barbarians and Greeks, Jews and Christians.
2.27: Moreover the wind is obedient to God, and fire to the messengers; the waters also to the demons and the earth to the sons of men.

3.1: Let us begin, then, with the Barbarians, and go on to the rest of the nations one after another,
3.2: that we may see which of them hold the truth as to God and which of them hold error.
3.3: The Barbarians, then, as they did not apprehend God, went astray among the elements,
3.4: and began to worship things created instead of their Creator;
3.5: and for this end they made images and shut them up in shrines, and behold, they worship them,
3.6: guarding them the while with much care, lest their gods be stolen by robbers.
3.7: And the Barbarians did not observe that that which acts as guard is greater than that which is guarded,
3.8: and that everyone who creates is greater than that which is created.
3.9: If it be, then, that their gods are too feeble to see to their own safety, how will they take thought for the safety of men?
3.10: Great then is the error into which the Barbarians wandered in worshiping lifeless images which can do nothing to help them.
3.11: And I am led to wonder, O King, at their philosophers, how that even they went astray, and gave the name of gods to images which were made in honor of the elements;
3.12: and that their sages did not perceive that the elements are also dissoluble and perishable.
3.13: For if a small part of an element is dissolved or destroyed, the whole of it may be dissolved and destroyed.
3.14: If then the elements themselves are dissolved and destroyed and forced to be subject to another that is more stubborn than they,
3.15: and if they are not in their nature gods, why, indeed, do they call the images which are made in their honor, God?
3.16: Great, then, is the error which the philosophers among them have brought on their followers.

4.1: Let us turn now, O King, to the elements in themselves,
4.2: that we may make clear in regard to them, that they are not gods, but a created thing, liable to ruin and change, which is of the same nature as man;
4.3: whereas God is imperishable and unvarying, and invisible, while yet He sees, and overrules, and transforms all things.
4.4: Those then who believe concerning the earth that it is a god have until now deceived themselves,
4.5: since it is furrowed and set with plants and trenched;
4.6: and it takes in the filthy refuse of men and beasts and cattle.
4.7: And at times it becomes unfruitful, for if it be burned to ashes it becomes devoid of life,
4.8: for nothing germinates from an earthen jar.
4.9: And besides if water be collected on it, it is dissolved together with its products.
4.10: And behold, it is trodden under foot of men and beast, and receives the blood of the slain;
4.11: and it is dug open, and filled with the dead, and becomes a tomb for corpses.
4.12: But it is impossible that a nature, which is holy and worthy and blessed and immortal, should allow of any one of these things.
4.13: And hence it appears to us that the earth is not a god but a creation of God.

5.1: In the same way, again, those erred who believed the waters to be gods.
5.2: For the waters were created for the use of man, and are put under his rule in many ways.
5.3: For they suffer change and admit impurity, and are destroyed and lose their nature while they are boiled into many substances.
5.4: And they take colors which do not belong to them;
5.5: they are also congealed by frost and are mingled and permeated with the filth of men and beasts, and with the blood of the slain.
5.6: And being checked by skilled workmen through the restraint of aqueducts, they flow and are diverted against their inclination,
5.7: and come into gardens and other places in order that they may be collected and issue forth as a means of fertility for man,
5.8: and that they may cleanse away every impurity and fulfill the service man requires from them.
5.9: For what reason it is impossible that the waters should be a god, but they are a work of God and a part of the world.
5.10: In like manner also they who believed that fire is a god erred to no slight extent.
5.11: For it, too, was created for the service of men, and is subject to them in many ways: in the preparation of meats, and as a means of casting metals, and for other ends of which your Majesty is aware.
5.12: At the same time it is quenched and extinguished in many ways.
5.13: Again they also erred who believed the motion of the winds to be a god.
5.14: For it is well known to us that those winds are under the dominion of another,
5.15: at times their motion increases, and at times it fails and ceases at the command of Him who controls them.
5.16: For they were created by God for the sake of men, in order to supply the necessity of trees and fruits and seeds;
5.17: and to bring over the sea ships which transport for men necessities and goods from places where they are found to places where they are not found;
5.18: and to govern the quarters of the world.
5.19: And as for itself, at times it increases and again abates;
5.20: and in one place brings help and in another causes disaster at the bidding of Him who rules it.
5.21: And mankind is also able by known means to confine and keep it in check in order that it may fulfill for them the service they require from it.
5.22: And of itself it does not have any authority at all.
5.23: And hence it is impossible that the winds should be called gods, but rather a thing made by God.

6.1: So also, they erred who believed that the sun is a god.
6.2: For we see that it is moved by the compulsion of another, and revolves and makes its journey,
6.3: and proceeds from sign to sign, rising and setting every day, so as to give warmth for the growth of plants and trees,
6.4: and to bring forth into the air with which it [(sunlight)] is mingled every growing thing which is on the earth.
6.5: And to it there belongs by comparison a part in common with the rest of the stars in its course;
6.6: and though it is one in its nature it is associated with many parts for the supply of the needs of men;
6.7: and that not according to its own will but rather according to the will of Him who rules it.
6.8: And hence it is impossible that the sun should be a god, but the work of God;
6.9: and in like manner also the moon and the stars.

7.1: And those who believed of the men of the past, that some of them were gods, they too were much mistaken.
7.2: For as you yourself allow, O King, man is constituted of the four elements, and of a soul and a spirit (and hence he is called a microcosm),
7.3: and without any one of these parts he could not consist.
7.4: He has a beginning and an end, and he is born and dies.
7.5: But God, as I said, has none of these things in His nature, but is uncreated and imperishable.
7.6: And hence it is not possible that we should set up man to be of the nature of God—
7.7: man, to whom at times when he looks for joy, there comes trouble, and when he looks for laughter there comes to him weeping,
7.8: who is wrathful and covetous and envious, with other defects as well.
7.9: And he is destroyed in many ways by the elements and also by the animals.
7.10: And hence, O King, we are bound to recognize the error of the Barbarians,
7.11: that thereby, since they did not find traces of the true God, they fell aside from the truth, and went after the desire of their imagination,
7.12: serving the perishable elements and lifeless images, and through their error not apprehending what the true God is.

8.1: Let us turn further to the Greeks also, that we may know what opinion they hold as to the true God.
8.2: The Greeks, then, because they are more subtle than the Barbarians, have gone further astray than the Barbarians;
8.3: inasmuch as they have introduced many fictitious gods, and have set up some of them as males and some as females;
8.4: and in that some of their gods were found who were adulterers, and did murder, and were deluded, and envious, and wrathful and passionate, and parricides, and thieves, and robbers.
8.5: And some of them, they say, were crippled and limped, and some were sorcerers, and some actually went mad, and some played on lyres, and some were given to roaming on the hills, and some even died, and some were struck dead by lightning, and some were made servants even to men, and some escaped by flight, and some were kidnapped by men, and some, indeed, were lamented and deplored by men.
